reviews.title: Will probably not last too long. Made in China.
reviews.text:
These are supercheap. . . albeit not inexpensive. . . Made in China shoes being sold by a Chinese company on Amazon.

They have a mesh upper, so they are breathable. There is some padding on the footbed. There is virtually no arch support. There is some padding around the ankle.

The sole is plastic. It is nonslip. There is some tread on the bottom, but not a lot. Probably enough tread to last the life of the shoes, which will probably not be longer than one season.

If you are the type of person who likes to exchange their shoes frequently, then the shoes can certainly work. Especially if you’re not expecting too much from them.

reviews.title: My First Pair of Slip On Sneakers
reviews.text:
I own more pairs of shoes right now that at any time in my life. Part of the reason is because I am involved in more activities than I was in the past and I am thus in need of more types of shoes. My desire to diversify further led me to get my feet inside a pair of INZCOU Men's Non-Slip Gym Sneakers.

I’ve owned lots of pairs of sneakers, but this is the first time I have owned sneakers that do not have a tongue. Instead, these shoes are slip- on and that makes them a little easier to put on because you don’t have to reach down and pull the tongue out, like you commonly do with other shoes. Once they are slipped into place, you tie and are ready to go.

These shoes fit about as expected. The only small issue I had was with the arch support in one of the shoes- it seemed a little off and it hurt at first. But after walking around for a while, everything is fine now. And since these are the first pair of black sneakers I have ever owned, they match up nicely with my darker jeans. It’s a welcome change from the white sneakers I usually wear.

It’s good to have a variety of shoes and INZCOU sneakers have proven a good addition to my collection. I will still wear traditional sneakers, the type that doesn’t slip on. But I am happy to add this set of sneakers to my growing supply.
reviews.author: AFERCDY2EFJKT7QUQ75GISNHTFOQ

Shoes Girls Kindly Note Have you measured your child’s feet recently? Selecting the correct size is important. Since each child’s foot is unique, no two children will have the same fit for any given shoe size. To make sure shoes are fitted correctly, both feet should be measured every time a new pair of shoes is bought. Proper fitting should always be done while standing, since feet spread out in this position. Toes should be able to wiggle quite freely and should not be restricted by the shoe. reviews.title: durable and comfortable
reviews.text: OK, so I was not expecting these to be comfortable for my 12.5hr shifts, nor was I expecting them to be durable. Surprise!! They are both. I am a nurse, enough said about comfort. I got poop on them, washed them in my washing machine, expecting to remove tattered and torn, I found them clean and INTACT!!!!! The color is just as bright as the day I  bought them. My coworkers are spending $150 or more on HOKA and I got these cheeeeap!! I love them!!!! Plus, when it rains they don't squeak????? I was so surprised, I don't know what to say about that. The heel is see thru and there isn't even any water inside after washing them. Sized very accurate, if anything they were 1/2 inch longer than expected. I wear a 7.5 in womens U.S. and these are not tight AT ALL, if I had gotten an 8, which I normally do when buying online and especially foreign, I would have been in trouble. Get them! I am getting another color as soon as I finish typing.
reviews.author: AE2P4IJZ4223YRSHYE4V6N73MLNQ

For the inexpensive price, will keep to wear in short stints.

reviews.title: Great shoe if you need to put inserts or orthotics inside
reviews.text: This shoe is a slip on, so you have to have a foot that can slip into it, order on the larger side for you to do this, or have a shoe horn.  It is not a supportive arch, but this works for me as I have orthotics that need to go inside of the shoe.  They are attractive.  If you are looking for a high arch shoe this is not the shoe for you, the inside of the shoe is flat and hard.  Just know that going in.  If you can't bend your foot or have a  high insole you may need a shoe horn, or need to wear thin socks.  But a great knock around shoe.

Ventilation ports add breathability and help feet cool and fresh. Lacing and Tongue: The shoe lacing is engineered to give extra stability. The long shoe tongue design is easy to take on and off, while adds a safety, energetic feel. Lightweight and Comfy Liner: Removable and soft insoles support the feet arches and it doesn't put too much pressure on your feet. Durable and Soft Outsole: The slip resistant EVA outsole made of stretchable MD material can be bent freely. A soft, comfortable elastane heel allows for natural movement of the feet. Occasion: Classic color matching designed, black&white is easy to match clothing. These women walking shoes are ideal for gym, running, workout, nursing, tennis or even gardening.

reviews.title: No breaking in!!
reviews.text: I purchased these for my daughter. She's on her feet all day in a bakery as an assistant pastry chef. Her old shoes literally fell apart. She's been wearing these for a week straight and has absolutely nothing to complain about. Her feet do not hurt when she's running around in this fast paced kitchen. The non-slip works (they grip well). She also just told me that she didn't have any breaking in period on these like she does other non slip shoes. As soon as she put them on and went to work, the comfort was there. I have yet to hear her complain her feet hurt since I purchased these for her.. So for that, I'm thankful. In her words, these shoes are awesome mom!! If possible, I will come back after she's worn them for a month or so to update on how they hold up. But comfort is everything in the business she works at!!
reviews.author: AFPP7JMNAFNTNGH5K43Z45OCFVKA

reviews.title: Super Comforting
reviews.text: I usually buy ASICS, I’m a senior with Osteoarthritis in my ankles & foot joints & need a sturdy shoe with good arch support & comfortable. But, I wanted a less expensive shoe to slip on when going out to the store or errands. When they arrived I thought I would put them on & walk around the house for a bit to decide if they would work for me. 3 hours later I still had them on & didn’t want to take them off.

All of these updates make the new GEL-VENTURE 8 shoe a recommended companion for your next adventure. Synthetic-and-mesh Imported lug-sole sole GEL Technology: Cushioning provides excellent shock absorption Re-engineered outsole lugs for better off-road traction: Reduced amount of overlays around the forefoot without sacrificing durability:

reviews.title: Absolutely Fabulous
reviews.text: Is this what new shoes feel like now?? I wore a pair I liked so much for 10 years,  still going,  and I guess I didn't realize that maybe over time the comfort level gradually faded and I didn't notice; these actually cushion my feet across the whole bottom and my feet stopped hurting as soon as I put them on! I have narrow feet and someone told me to try this brand which I never wore before.  I have a spring in my step and much better balance; i carried 3 large packages up a curb at UPS. That's something I couldn't have done yesterday.  (I have neuropathy and poor balance and my feet are numb with weak leg muscles.) I'm so much more capable now.  Before I avoided standing up.  Plus I got a great deal and I like the color.  Asics is my new goto brand.

reviews.title: Good comfortable everyday shoes
reviews.text: Adidas memory foam sneakers are my absolute favorite for everyday wear. I doubt these would be supportive enough for running, but they’re great for walking and day-to-day use. I walked probably 10+ miles over two days wearing these shoes right out of the box with no break-in, and they felt great. My only complaint is that they don’t seem to last as long as some other sneakers - for me, the tread wears down considerably and the white soles get yucky within just a few months. But, for the price, I think they’re worth it

title: ASICS Mens Gel-Venture 6 Running Shoe
description: The GEL-Venture 5 provides great fit and everyday comfort, with Rearfoot GEL Cushioning and a rugged outsole ideal for a variety of terrains. In 1949, Mr. Kihachiro Onitsuka began his athletic footwear company (Onitsuka Co., Ltd.) by manufacturing basketball shoes out of his living room in Kobe, Japan. He chose the name ASICS for his company in 1977, based on a famous Latin phrase "Anima Sana In Corpore Sano," which, when translated, expresses the ancient ideal of "A Sound Mind in a Sound Body." Taking the acronym of this phrase, ASICS was founded on the belief that the best way to create a healthy and happy lifestyle is to promote total health and fitness. Today, ASICS offers a full line of performance-driven athletic shoes and technical active sports apparel and accessories dedicated to bringing harmony to the body and soul. Rubber sole Outdoor-ready runner with mesh and brushstroke-patterned underlays Rearfoot GEL cushioning Removable sockliner accommodates medical orthotics Trail-specific outsole with reversed traction lugs AHAR outsole rubber in critical high-wear areas

reviews.title: Pleasantly surprised!
reviews.text:
I wasn’t expecting these to work, I usually get Innov8’s but they are >$100 and stopped carrying my women’s size 12, so I was searching for another option. I got Mens 11 and even though I have extremely narrow women’s feet, these work very well. The toe box is wider than it needs to be on me, but the laces and strict of the shoe keeps my feet stable. I actually like the connected tongue as well.

I use these mainly for trail walking (5-10 miles a week) and gym workouts.

To sum it up, these are great quality and performance barefoot style shoes that are a surprisingly good price! I love how my foot feels connected with the ground but still has enough cushion to make it comfortable.
reviews.author: AHIXGREYZQKKBNWWXCZJUBUUKRUA

reviews.title: Amazing!
reviews.text:
I won't use any other shoe for working out from now on. Most workout shoes I've tried were cushioned nicely but with a thick, high sole my feet tended to bend in weird ways. These are by far the most comfortable and practical shoe I've ever worn, and I like being closer to the ground. They've held up pretty well so far but I haven't done a whole lot of hiking in them yet, mostly gym use. What hiking I have done has been great, very comfortable, and no issues.

They're a great price as well for someone like me who is pretty hard on shoes and goes trough them fairly often, usually a pair every 1-2 years when it's workout/hiking shoes.

reviews.title: A comfortable shoe for casual use
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:1015be58bd6250fd72bf29d4d5aef0af]] This shoe is very comfortable and the stitching and construction are very good.  I like how my feet feel snug in this shoe.  The high back heel support and high shoe tongue helps to cradle my foot.  I really like this shoe.  However, I do Not see this shoe as a replacement for an athletic sneaker.  The material used on the sole is all foam.  It will not withstand high athletic use.  For example, pavement basketball and running will tear up these bottoms really quick.  I really wish that they would have made this shoe with a rubber bottom.  The non-slip sole description is limited at best.  Nonetheless, I like this shoe and use it for casual purposes and work.

reviews.title: Runs Small
reviews.text:
These are great sneakers if you choose the right size. The exterior fabric is lightweight and breathable. The mesh allows for good air flow. This also makes the fabric quicker to dry if it gets wet. The insoles have good arch support and great overall cushioning. The soles have excellent traction on any surface. The sizing runs a few centimeters short of standard US sizing. If you choose your normal size, your toes may be a little cramped for space. If you prefer a little breathing room, size up. These shoes are good for all types of exercise and for streetwear. I highly recommend this product.

Thanks so much for taking the time to read this review. I hope the information that I've provided makes your decision a little easier.

Extremely comfortable sneaker, lightweight and flexible but still offers good support for daily activities. I love that the sole is flexible because it allows me to "feel" the floor. I was a dancer in my youth and learned to work with the floor and grip with my toes, this shoe allows me to feel the floor so I can steady my balance accordingly, yet it's still strong enough to offer adequate protection from outside "hazards" such as gravel. I'd definitely buy it again.

reviews.title: Overall, comfortable
reviews.text: These are odd shoes, the shape and the colors clash. There's no stitching or closure to the edges on the tongue so it's just bare foam which looks cheap. The top part has a bunch of different textures and fabrics, plastic, leather, cloth, suede, foam, very thin jacket like material. I don't like that it looks like it's pulling up by the time box, like it will separate. If you normally wear no show socks you might want to use a fuller coverage pair with these. I had some uncomfortable rubbing at my ankle and the top of my foot from the weird seams inside. The shoe is comfortable to stand and walk in and the sole is pretty grippy.

Zero-drop, non-elevated sole for proper posture and balance. Sticky-grip rubber outsole combines grip, traction, and durability for trail-ready performance.

reviews.title: Flexible but protective
reviews.text: Feels like other barefoot shoes but with some slightly thicker soles. The shape still feels like zero heel height shoes but with a little more material between you and the ground which is kind of nice if you walk on rocky or irregular surfaces. Soles are grippy but make that "basketball court" squeak on smooth surfaces sometimes. The uppers are breathable and comfortable and the tension is around the midfoot which feels good. You hardly feel anything around the ankle which is nice. The toe spread is moderate. If you have normal width feet you'll probably be ok but if you tend to favor wider shoes it feels like how normal width should feel, but at no point does my foot roll over the sole. They fit kind of tight but after a few days of wear they loosened up. I usually wear 11.5 and ordered an 11, after that initial break-in, they feel fine.
reviews.author: AHD7XKMVFK6GKLHU5X7WW53URF2A

reviews.title: Nice design and price. Great work out shoe.
reviews.text: This is a well made shoe for the money. Goes on nicely, fits great, and I like the looks. If there are any cons it would be that when I ride my bike there isn’t any firm support, and need to adjust my foot positioning frequently. I may need to get another pair of she’s for riding.  Otherwise, I would give it a 10 on a 10 scale, being extremely comfortable.
reviews.author: AHL46SRUUMDXSTTKGI5FXEEKRKMA

reviews.title: Quality, minimal, and great shoe
reviews.text:
Great minimalist shoe that is high quality and is very comfortable! Not the best if you want to do athletic movements from side to side as your foot tends to roll but is great if you don't overdo it! I've had it for months and they've held up awesome. Was skeptical about the brand but it has turned out to be my favorite shoe; all my 100+ Brooks, Asics, Nikes, etc sit unused now. My Achilles don't ache anymore after using this!

Be wary to ease into using these or you might get injured if you're used to padded shoes. Overall I would definitely recommend these to anyone looking into affordable but quality shoes!

Imported Rubber sole

reviews.title: CURRENTLY have FOUR pairs of the Cohesion 11!
reviews.text:
EDIT: Just bought three more pairs in different colors of the Cohesion 11. They are NOT on Saucony's site any longer and the ONLY place I found them in all the colors I wanted was on Amazon from ORVA.
I am stocking up because I LOVE these shoes because they FIT my less than perfect 70 year old feet, bunions and all, and once they're gone, they're gone. No longer making them as they have moved on to revised versions.

I TRIED the Cohesion 10. Didn't like and returned. I read reviews of the 12, 13 and 14 and as the Cohesion 11's work SO well for my needs and fit perfectly, I will stay with these (and add a couple MORE pairs!)
________________________________________________________________________________________

First, I turn 70 on my next bd. I have been a Saucony fan, ONLY athletic shoe I buy, for DECADES.

These didn't disappoint.

PROS:

TOE BED - I have bunions, I broke one of my toes, so it now sticks up a bit. Not perfect feet, which is not surprising for someone turning 70. LOVE the TOE BED. Roomy, without feeling TOO roomy.

SIZE - I find these TRUE TO SIZE. Reading reviews for almost ANY shoe, you see some saying TOO BIG! some saying TOO SMALL! Well, I have worn an 8.5 in any type of athletic shoe for decades and went with that. A size smaller or larger would NOT have fit.

FABRIC - I was concerned the MESH would feel really rough. I ALWAYS wear with a short sport sock, the kind not visible, but thought the mesh might still RUB. I have had on about an hour, just walking around in the house before I decide these are a keeper and the MESH inside and out feels soft and giving, LIKE THAT VERY MUCH.

LOOKS - FIRST, I got the ALL black as I wanted to wear with my black jeans, ALL black is always dressier and doesn't stand out as a running shoe if you want something a little dressier, but also got another pair as a running shoe, little fancier re color with same fit and comfort.

PRICE - couldn't beat the price. Even lower end Saucony (and Saucony has been my brand of choice for DECADES) are not CHEAP, but got this on sale for about 39 bucks, with tax about 41. SEEM like a high quality shoe if it had cost more.

CONS:

NOT for really HARDCORE, 30 mile a week RUNNERS: These are GREAT for my purpose. Doing maybe 3 miles three or four times a week, I also rotate shoes, these aren't my only pair. The way these are designed, I don't think they would suit someone who runs five-six miles almost every day. For mild, moderate runner/joggers, I think these are a great buy.

SHOELACES: This is minor and I would still have gotten these shoes. Getting another pair of black shoelaces, not a biggie, but surprised that are SO thin and flimsy. I am not one who likes the LOOOOONG shoelaces that so many athletic shoes seem to come with, and these are a bit short, but fine. BUT FLIMSY for athletic laces.

BOTTOMLINE: FIVE STARS. Would buy these again and the price just made it better! IF YOU GET SHIPPED THRU AMAZON and ESP if you're a PRIME member like I am, you get free ship and free return ANYWAY. SO IF you've been wondering about these after reading reviews, GIVE EM A TRY, ESP if they're on sale! You can return if they don't fit.

reviews.title: FIT OUT OF THE BOX...as USUAL!  ONE CAVEAT
reviews.text:
Been wearing Sauconys since my running days, decades ago. I'm now 70 and Saucony still fit me the best even with bunions and I broke a toe that sticks up a little.

They have always been wear out of the box shoes for me and give me the support I need.

IF I was still a runner, I would upgrade, but for my needs, doing a lot of walking, all day comfort, these are perfect and at a GREAT price.

I already have two pairs in different colors, plan on getting a couple more!

ONLY CAVEAT: laces are kind of wimpy and I'm going to replace, but that's SO MINOR, not taking a star off.

BOTTOMLINE: FIVE STARS for fit, quality, comfort and price.

reviews.title: Lack of Mid-Sole Support?
reviews.text:
I purchased these after buying TWO pairs of 'Fila's that were on sale on JC Penny's
and found them 'functional but could be better" .

The reason for buying shoes from this user is for walking- and by that I mean walking about 7
miles every morning and often 3-5 miles later in the day as well.

The pair I have liked is a DOUBLE FUSION by Nike that provides soft but firm support for the entire foot
from HEAL to TOE and Mid-Sole. The Fila's just feel 'flat' and do not provide the support or cushion
this user needs since I have no cartilage left in my knees and hips. Therefore, cushioning and support both are of prime importance (I'm a former long distance runner who averaged over 100 miles/week at one point).

The Saucony I read averages 'small' so best to order 1/2 size larger than one's usual (that is what I read in comments)...so that is what I did. Must say, size wise (width/length) of this shoe in doing so, couldn't be much better. My only complaint on this end is the mid-sole feels a bit 'flat or hard' as I'd hope it would be.
I did purchase at the Dollar Store (of all places) this extra thick insole to line along the bottom of the shoe
and that made a big difference.

title: ASICS Men's Gel-Venture 7 Running Shoes
description: Take on the toughest terrain in confidence with the men's GEL-VENTURE 7 performance trail running shoe by ASICS. Featuring GEL technology and an EVA midsole, this shoe is made for athletes who love to run in the great outdoors. GEL technology in the rearfoot area of the shoe provides shock absorption where you need it most, while the ORTHOLITE sockliner provides an additional layer of cushioning underfoot. It even molds to the shape of your feet, making each run even more comfortable than the last. Extra support comes from the sturdy upper crafted from synthetic leather, while the internal heel counter gently helps your foot remain in its natural line of motion, making for a more efficient stride. Meanwhile the EVA midsole protects your feet against impact and increases rebound, giving you an energetic feeling as you clock up the miles. Durable and practical, the GEL-VENTURE 7 shoe makes an excellent training partner whether you're new to trail running or a seasoned pro. Synthetic-and-mesh Imported Ethylene Vinyl Acetate sole Ortholite X-40 Sockliner: This premium sockliner features higher rebound properties while providing excellent moisture management and a high-level of breathability (Ortholite is a registered trademark of O2 Partners LLC.) Rearfoot GEL technology cushioning system: Attenuates shock during impact phase and allows for a smooth transition to midstance. Ortholite X-40 Sockliner: This premium sockliner features higher rebound properties while providing excellent moisture management and a high-level of breathability (Ortholite is a registered trademark of O2 Partners LLC.) Rearfoot GEL technology cushioning system: Attenuates shock during impact phase and allows for a smooth transition to midstance. Trail Specific Outsole: Reversed lugs provide uphill and downhill traction on all types of terrain.

title: Reebok Men's Bb4500 Hi 2 Sneaker
description: Reebok Men's Royal Bb4500 Hi2 Sneaker helps you show your respect for the game while keeping classic, everyday style on point. These mid-cut, basketball-inspired men's shoes are sure to be an instant favorite, with a blended leather and mesh upper weighing in for heritage comfort. Plus, a removable sockliner helps keep feet happy all day long. Reebok was founded for one of the best reasons possible: athletes wanted to run faster. So in the 1890s, Joseph William Foster made some of the first known running shoes with spikes in them. By 1895, he was in business making shoes by hand for top runners; and before long his fledgling company, J.W. reviews.title: Love Saucony's But Sizing Has Changed
reviews.text: I have normal to high-arched kayaks, so it's not easy for me to find shoes that fit properly.  Saucony tends to run narrow which I'm grateful for.  I've noticed, however, that the sizing (not just on these but everything) has become unreliable.  My last pair (purchased only 2 years ago) were a 10.5 and fit great...room in the toe box....and width was perfect WITHOUT an orthotic.  So I figured buying another 10.5 would be a no-brainer, right?  Nope.  The new 10.5's are shorter in the toe box (I was able to easily compare them), and the overall fit of the shoe seems wider.  So I had to exchange them for 11's.  Egads!  I've never been an 11!.  They have more room in the toe box, but they're also a bit wide for me.  However, I'm able to make them work by removing the insoles (yes, they're removable) and using my own (Superfeet Green ) + a bit thicker socks.  This seems to elevate my foot so I can compensate for the unwanted width.  It also makes them more cushioned and supportive.
reviews.author: AEMRW74WOEBX33JMZZV47XITIEEQ

reviews.title: Runs slightly smaller than my other shoes
reviews.text:
I walk between 55-65mi a week and wore through my previous pair of Under Armour Charged Assert 9 shoes in 2mo flat. I was searching for a running shoe under $50 offered in 4E width to replace them with.

I came across these Asics and was very excited to try them out. They're very lightweight and have pretty good grip, though I haven't stepped through any water on asphalt yet. They run slightly smaller than my previous pairs of shoes, I'd recommend going a half size up if you're worried about the sizing. These are snug and I have the laces backed out pretty far, but I'm sure after a few days, they'll be worn in a bit.

The heel cup has a lot of padding, which is comfortable. Overall, I think these are a pretty good value for the cost, I'm hoping they stretch out a bit with more wear. I do think they have a lot more support initially, I'm hoping it retains throughout my time with this pair of shoes.

reviews.title: Better than I expected
reviews.text:
My wife recently purchased me these sneakers for work when she noticed how worn my Others were. I'll be honest, I wasn't expecting to like the TEAL when she first showed them to me. But, after they arrived and they were worn to work, I was surprised by how many people were complementing the color. That got me a 'told ya so' from her when I got home.
The shoes are simple and straightforward. You can slip them on and off without tying the ties. A synthetic mesh upper with a thin insole is pretty much all the shoe is. VERY lightweight, feeling more like house shoes that sneakers.
The sole is a hard plastic, the offers a different kind support. A little more ridged than normal. After you've walked around in them you'll see how light and comfortable they are. Enjoy

reviews.title: These are GREAT if you have ogre feet! 🦍
reviews.text:
I bought two pairs in like-new condition via Amazon in 12 X-wide as I have all my other Skechers. They arrived in exactly new condition, and as soon as I took my first step in them I knew why they were so readily available. Though they were the right length and width for my big feet, the upper is so large that when I would take a step, it was like wearing ill-fitting flip flops and my feet came out of the shoes so far my heel was completely outside the shoe. My feet would have to be at least half-again as big around for these to fit properly. I feel like someone punked me and gave me the shoes that belonged to that green ogre from the Dreamworks movie series. These are the worst-fitting Skechers I’ve ever bought (the other bad ones have been too narrow). I went to a physical store today and bought a new pair in a different model but the same size that fits properly.

Same quality shoe, horrible fit, weird sole, returned immediately.

reviews.title: WHY TWO stars when I LOVE Saucony brand
reviews.text:
I have multiple pairs of Saucony. I wear them for working out, walking, running errands. Unless I am getting dressed up, my shoe of choice (and have been since 1990's when I realized they were a better fit, out of the box, than any of the other leading brands).

My Experience:

FIT - still fit out of the box. I wear an 8.5 in Saucony's and no matter which style I get, they fit. HOWEVER, the left insole is considerably higher than right. Just not comfortable and you're constantly aware of it.

COMFORT - OK comparing these to the Cohesion 11 (which I have two pairs of), not nearly as much comfort, cushion. And like I mentioned, left insole higher, so just weird and wonky walking or running in these.

QUALITY - overall, quality seems higher on the Cohesion 11, just looking at upper shoe, and how much more cushion the 11 has. So lower quality than the 11 overall.

COLOR - The purple color is WHY I ordered these over the Cohesion 11. I figured the 10 would be similar and the 10 had the GREAT purple color, the 11 didn't. But it isn't the color that is what you're walking on!

PRICE - EVEN though less cushion than the Cohesion 11, I would have kept them if the insole in left wasn't so different than right.

BOTTOMLINE: After this, will stick with the Cohesion 11 (or may try 12 or 13, I look at reviews). The 11 I have been really happy with. So if unless you're a hardcore jogger logging lots of miles, I would think the 11 would suffice for you. The 10, not so much.

reviews.title: Disappointing “Versafoam”
reviews.text:
First of all, Saucony is my favorite tennis shoe brand to wear. I’ve owned lots of pairs of tennis shoes made by Saucony from running shoes to jazz dance shoes and they have always been my go-to Brand for tennis shoes.
As I have gotten older, I am noticing that my feet hurt more and more, I own a greenhouse nursery and am on my feet all day, almost everyday. I’m noticing some pains in my feet that I didn’t have before. Most of my jobs throughout my adulthood have been jobs where I had to stand/walk a lot so I’m no stranger to foot fatigue.
Being that these shoes are labeled “Versafoam” I assumed they would have arch support and feel like walking on a light, squishy cloud. NO, NO, NO!!
For some odd reason, Saucony has used an extremely thin insert (see picture) and underneath the insert is a HARD platform. Yes, the sole of this tennis shoe is Versafoam, but what good is this foam when you use a hard platform on top of the foam?!?! So, you’re basically walking on a very thin insert which is on top of a hard platform which is on top of Versafoam.
Poor design and definitely not what I had expected in a Saucony shoe.
I had high hopes for these tennies but sadly am disappointed that they are not comfortable like I would expect a Versafoam type shoe to be.
The shoe looks great and fits true to size, it just falls short on the comfort level.
I am sending them back :(
Here are my stats: 5’4” 135 lbs., athletic build and active.
reviews.author: AFPOTRIEA47XTET3BA7EMZLDCLDA

reviews.title: Coming apart &Not true to American sizes
reviews.text:
THese finally worked for me after trying Three times.
I wear an 11W normally in American sizes.
You know, the scale you put your foot on to get the size you need.
After an 11 wouldn't accept my foot - I returned them.
Ordered a 12W. That was WAY Too Tight. But long enough.
I got this size 13XW in May. It was a little tight in the width. BUT, it was as a new pair normally fits.
A little tight. Then they shape themselves to your foot. Right?
All was well until a few days ago. I stepped into a hole in the yard early morning. Between the wet grass and that hole being filled with water. My shoes are now soaking wet.
I let them dry in the sun but now they stink. I use Desonex regularly so this was surprising to me.
Into the washer they go. With some old towels and run on regular wash.
There was nothing wrong with these shoes before their bath.
These pictures are what they look like after being washed ONE Time?
The Fau-Leather is separating at the stitching. There is not a hole yet but it is Very thin now.
That is a high stress ares that flexes with each step. If these shoes can not handle One trip through the washing machine now? How do I keep buying them?
I love that they are really light. I have been wearing them for years.
But something has changed lately. The last pair made it 143 month before the sole separated and wore thru the middle. I do not have a hard step. Shoes wear normally across the entire sole.
Materials have changed. Sizing has changed.
Once you find the correct size for you they fit nicely.
Just Very Cheaply made now. Originally I would get them at Walmart. But suddenly they didn't fit and they couldn't get me the right size there.
So I looked online.
Way to ruin a good shoe Avia
I'm no longer looking at you
reviews.author: AFIDPK57IMPFMJGMERCGBWOMJTVQ

description: Rubber sole Soft Vegan Suede Upper Double functional zippers for easy on / off Memory Foam Insoles for extra comfort Durable long lasting outsole 1 Inch hidden wedge for added height

reviews.title: Comfy and Stylish
reviews.text:
I ordered these sneakers in an 8.5 and an 8.5 wide. I normally wear an 8.5, sometimes a 9 and have slightly wide feet. With and without socks the 8.5 feel great. The 8.5 wide are slightly more roomy in the toe area but a little too loose when I walk. I'm leaning towards the 8.5 regular width. The footbed is nicely cushioned and the heel inside is not too high. The zippers are on both sides of each shoe and both are functional. I look forward to styling these with many outfits.

Update: I've worn them a couple of times now and I can confirm that they are pretty comfortable even after a couple hours of continuous wear. They are easy to put on and go. Only downside I am noticing is that the white sole is getting dirty pretty quickly so I will have to clean them regularly.
reviews.author: AGXAXJEWX52WIIK5J3AYQCIVEULA

reviews.title: Review from a person with difficult feet.
reviews.text: Let me preface this with the fact that I have lots of feet problems—extremely high arches, plantar fasciitis, supination etc. I do not feel i have wide feet but wides are often more comfortable so I was happy to try my normal size (8.5) in wide. The length is standard. For the price they are cute and pretty comfy overall. I do not feel the claim of memory foam provides any more comfort. The foam is so thin they feel like normal (if not slightly poorer quality) shoes. Even with the wide width I feel pressure just below the zipper on the inside of my foot. I am unsure if that will release as the shoes are worn. They are easy to get on/off and the double zippers help with my high arches. (Normally this style of shoe is really hard for me to get on because of the old arches.)  I am undecided if I will keep them yet because of the pressure near the zipper but if you have normal feet these shoes might just be acceptable for you. If you have wide feet and no other issues they may not be wide enough.
reviews.author: AFYMLJPONGXJQXGQ2ZDALR3TR4VA

reviews.title: Too Tight
reviews.text: Like many products made in China, sizes seem to be random. I normally wear a medium width, but needed a wide shoe after foot surgery. In the picture you can see how much narrower this boot is than a 9 1/2 width sneaker. The faux suede captured every piece of lint nearby, but I would have tolerated that if the shoe had been wide as described.

reviews.title: Fits well; love the style

I used to be a 50-60miles per week runner, and personally, i had to avoid these type of flattish-arched shoes or my feet would get sore / injured quickly. title: ASICS Women's Gel-Excite 6 Running Shoes
description: Enjoy the freedom of running on the open road with the women's GEL-EXCITE 6 running shoe, specifically designed to help you go the distance. Featuring GEL technology to the rear for comfort and bounce with every step, this ASICS running shoe with its high-level of energized cushioning is sure to keep you going even across the most challenging distances. Designed with Jacquard mesh fabric to allow the feet to stay cool and dry even when working up a sweat, this men's running shoe will keep your feet fresher and in top condition for longer. Set on a durable outsole for a hardwearing quality, it features AMPLIFOAM technology for a flexible and tactile ride. Lightweight for easier running, its ORTHOLITE sockliner also helps with effective moisture management while providing responsive rebound to improve your overall technique. Packaged neatly in a contemporary design, with signature ASICS stripes thrown into the mix, the GEL-EXCITE 6 model is a versatile footwear option that puts your comfort first. Weight: 8.35. Heel Height: 20mm. Forefoot Height: 12mm. Synthetic-and-mesh Rubber sole AmpliFoam Midsole - Engineered to maintain durability at softer densities, providing better flexibility, comfort, and platform adaptability ideal for natural running Rearfoot GEL technology cushioning system - Attenuates shock during impact phase and allows for a smooth transition to midstance Ortholite Sockliner - Moisture management (Ortholite is a registered trademark of ATP Manufacturing LLC)

title: New Balance Men's 520 V5 Running Shoe
description: A great look combines with amazing features and useful technology in the New Balance 520v5, providing you with a versatile pair of running shoes. Complete with an NB Comfort Insert, a durable rubber outsole with underfoot flex grooves, and a thick injection-molded EVA midsole that's plush and offers data-inspired cushioning, these athletic shoes are designed for a soft feeling that lasts all day. Plus, the supportive synthetic and mesh upper offers lightweight support your feet are sure to love. All that amazing technology and design is included in a shoe that looks equally as great as it performs, with a stylish design and classic New Balance branding that will keep heads turning whether you're heading into the gym, onto the track, or out with friends. 50% Synthetic, 50% Mesh Imported Rubber sole Underfoot Comfort: These running shoes feature a lightweight injection-molded EVA foam midsole to provide lightweight support, plus an NB Comfort Insert to offer additional cushioning Supportive Design: The breathable mesh and synthetic uppers on these fitness shoes are designed to be soft and supportive on the feet. The upper sits atop a durable rubber outsole Versatile and Lightweight Running Shoes: Whether you're working through tough training sessions or a busy weekend of errands, these New Balance running shoes are designed to provide all-day comfort A Great Finishing Touch to Any Outfit: With a stylish silhouette and classic NB branding, these cushioned running shoes can be paired with any casual or athletic outfit without looking out of place Comfortable Running Shoes: These synthetic running shoes deliver a plush feeling that lasts, thanks to the underfoot flex grooves and data-inspired comfort technology for enhanced all-day comfort

title: Fila Women's Disruptor II Sneaker
description: Amazon.com This lightweight, retro-inspired sneaker from Fila is perfect for every day and then some. Made with a durable nubuck upper with a smooth, soft finish, this shoe features a removable cushioned insole and an extra-thick rubber sole for excellent shock absorption. A padded tongue and collar and soft lining keep feet feeling fresh and comfortable throughout the day. From the Manufacturer Fila began in 1911 in the small alpine town of Biella, Italy, a place renowned for manufacturing the world's finest textiles and weaving fabrics with impeccable quality, true innovation, and extraordinary beauty. Born of the city and its traditions, Fila is inspired by Biella's dedication to artistry, luxury, and elegance. It's Fila's birthright, it's in their soul, and it defines their future. They continue to enrich the athletic experience by improving comfort and performance with Italian design, quality craftsmanship, and refined materials. There is no feeling quite as luxurious as that of playing in Fila, and that is the reason for Fila's existence. They create what is desired, not just what is necessary. Take your non-traditional, society-rejecting ways beyond the track with the Fila Disruptor II Premium. Lightweight leather and synthetic upper. Traditional lace-up closure. Breathable mesh lining. Padded insole.

title: ASICS Gel-Excite 4 Women's Running Shoe
description: Comfort takes the lead with the new gel-excite™ 4 shoe. Featuring rearfoot gel® cushioning technology, the gel-excite™ 4 shoe is the latest development in an energetic running shoe that is versatile enough for moderate mileage and all day use. In 1949, Mr. Kihachiro Onitsuka began his athletic footwear company (Onitsuka Co., Ltd.) by manufacturing basketball shoes out of his living room in Kobe, Japan. He chose the name ASICS for his company in 1977, based on a famous Latin phrase "Anima Sana In Corpore Sano," which, when translated, expresses the ancient ideal of "A Sound Mind in a Sound Body." Taking the acronym of this phrase, ASICS was founded on the belief that the best way to create a healthy and happy lifestyle is to promote total health and fitness. Today, ASICS offers a full line of performance-driven athletic shoes and technical active sports apparel and accessories dedicated to bringing harmony to the body and soul. 100% Synthetic Imported Rubber sole Rearfoot GEL Cushioning System: Attenuates shock during impact phase and allows for a smooth transition to midstance. Removable Sockliner: A sockliner which can be removed to accommodate a medical orthotic. AHAR Outsole: Acronym for ASICS High Abrasion Rubber. Placed in critical areas of the outsole for exceptional durability. Reflectivity

reviews.title: UPDATE!! These shoes KILL 😭
reviews.text:
UPDATED REVIEW: If you HATE your feet... buy these shoes. If you're a masochist... buy these shoes. Except, whatever you do, DO NOT BUY THESE SHOES!!!

Unfortunately, I have wore these shoes *very* little up until my Returns period ran out. Maybe 5 minutes to walk down to the mailbox or 30 minutes to drive to town and pick up a grocery order. Never once did I wear them to walk in for more than, literally, 10 minutes. And the discomfort I attributed to 'breaking them in'... NOPE. I was working out in the yard today - second Autumn day here in Georgia, so I've put all sandals and flip flops away for outdoors - and both days I have legitimately had to come inside and take the shoes off because my left foot was in excruciating pain. The right shoe has never been as uncomfortable as the left, so it is possible I've gotten a defective pair. However, given that I have never personally owned Asics before, even if I could return/exchange, I would not try again.

Also, my feet cramped like a mutha🤬 when I laid down in bed last night. (Ladies, if you've ever worn extremely cheapo flats in your life, and you have any semblance of an arch, you'll know what I'm talking about).

-10 ⭐️ If I could!!

4.5 Stars, but I rounded up -

They're not as comfortable as Skechers, more comfortable than Saucony. Nice enough looking on, with shorts and leggings, without being clunky (some shoes just look clunky when wearing shorts or the fitted leggings, IMO, if you have normal sized calves and wear a size 9 like I do). I don't know why, but the lack of a )( on top of the mesh, at the toes, where it would run from the rubber toe point to the laces section, doesn't look good. Again, I do not know why that bothers me but it bothers me bad enough every time I wear them that it almost makes me not like (the aesthetics of) the shoe. It makes that section look really broad, like I have big & broad ties with a narrow heel 🤷🏼‍♀️ and I don't. I'm probably just being picky.

reviews.title: Great shoe, colors a bit off
reviews.text:
The blue is a beautiful blue - see pic..their pic is lighter, reality it’s very dark - pretty, but with my light skin it’s too dark. The muted gold is also a bit more pronounced in real, approaching cheesy, but it’s ok.

The gray and white are actually gray and lavender. Made so light in the pic I thought it was white stripes. It’s straight up medium lavender. Not light lavender as pictured. Not for me. Both go back, but I found a cream color on ASICS website.

They’re very comfortable and give lots of support for the very good price ASICS always have. I need more arch support but just a little. My feet rotate inwards just a bit, but they’re quite good for an average sneaker!

They run as off in size as most sneakers do these days. I’m a 7.5 in all shoes for decades, but I’m an 8.5, at least, in running shoes or cross trainers. So size up a full size for sure.

A solid shoe! A terrific price.

reviews.title: BEST shoe for wider feet and bunions
reviews.text: I wear these for walking and running. I have extremely wide feet and a pretty severe bunion on my right foot. Not only do they NOT hurt or make my foot go numb, they’re actually very comfortable and supportive! title: New Balance Men's 510 V5 Trail Running Shoe
description: Are you looking for a lightweight, durable and supportive pair of trail running shoes? Then your search ends with the New Balance 510v5. Durability on the outside meets responsive comfort inside for a lightweight running shoe that's as ready as you are for a trail run or whatever else your day has in store. Built with a rugged construction, these New Balance trail shoes offer relentless durability that is suitable for various runs both on- and off-road. The lugged outsole on these comfortable running shoes features AT TREAD technology which provides enhanced traction on the trail or the road, while the lightweight injection-molded EVA foam midsole includes both ABZORB technology for impact absorption and an NB Soft + Supportive Insert for memory comfort that lasts. For added protection, the reinforced Toe Protect tip helps protect your toes from rocks, roots and debris so you can go off-road with confidence. 50% Synthetic, 50% Textile Imported Rubber sole Cushioned Running Shoes: ABZORB midsole technology in this runner absorbs impact through a combination of cushioning and compression resistance, and the rubber outsole provides strength and durability Built-In Off-Road Technology: Toe Protect technology helps protect feet from rocks, roots and debris, while AT Tread on the outsole provides versatile traction during both on- and off-road activities Plush, Cushioned Running Shoes: These runners feature lightweight injection-molded EVA foam midsoles to provide lightweight cushioning and an NB Soft + Supportive Insert for memory comfort that lasts Built to Last: With an all-over durable construction and an aggressive lugged outsole, these trail running shoes are designed to stand up to all of your outdoor activities Athletic Aesthetic: With synthetic and mesh uppers that deliver breathability and a top-quality fit, these running shoes also boast a stylish look to fit in with your active lifestyle

The company's success stems from it's high quality, diversified, and affordable product line that meets consumers' Various lifestyle needs. Since it's inception in 1992, the Skechers diverse product offering has grown from utility style boots to include seven Skechers brands and five uniquely branded fashion lines for men and women. Skechers is an award-winning global leader in lifestyle footwear offering shoes that appeal to trend-savvy men, women and kids everywhere. The brand's styles include the latest innovative athletic, casual and fashion sneakers as well as sandals and boots—with many collections featuring Skechers air-cooled memory foam insoles for added comfort. Plus, Skechers offers a range of slip-resistant work FOOTWEAR for men and women, as well as cool, fun, playful and lighted styles that boys and girls love. title: ASICS Women's Gel-Venture 5 Running Shoes
description: The GEL-Venture 5 provides great fit and everyday comfort, with Rearfoot GEL Cushioning System and a rugged outsole ideal for a variety of terrains. In 1949, Mr. Kihachiro Onitsuka began his athletic footwear company (Onitsuka Co., Ltd.) by manufacturing basketball shoes out of his living room in Kobe, Japan. He chose the name ASICS for his company in 1977, based on a famous Latin phrase "Anima Sana In Corpore Sano," which, when translated, expresses the ancient ideal of "A Sound Mind in a Sound Body." Taking the acronym of this phrase, ASICS was founded on the belief that the best way to create a healthy and happy lifestyle is to promote total health and fitness. Today, ASICS offers a full line of performance-driven athletic shoes and technical active sports apparel and accessories dedicated to bringing harmony to the body and soul. 100% Synthetic Imported Rubber sole Rearfoot GEL Cushioning System: Attenuates shock during impact phase and allows for a smooth transition to midstance. Removable Sockliner: A sockliner which can be removed to accommodate a medical orthotic. Trail Specific Outsole: Reversed lugs provide uphill and downhill traction on all types of terrain. AHAR Outsole: Acronym for ASICS High Abrasion Rubber. Placed in critical areas of the outsole for exceptional durability.

reviews.title: Great shoe!

reviews.title: so promising and yet so disappointing
reviews.text: I hate that I hate these shoes. They look super cute, fit very well, feel very cushiony (at first) but unfortunately they cause me a lot of pain. I bought these for running after my sister recommended I try Asics. Over the last 2 years I've bought 5 pairs of running shoes and all of them cause different kinds of pain. These ones give me the worst pain in the ball of my foot, which then stretches into my toes, which then go numb - and all of this happened on a two mile walk. I didn't even begin running yet. Maybe it's my feet but I'm disappointed that I have yet another pair of shoes that I can only wear for exercises that require me to stand in place because even on the elliptical they are useless after about 15 minutes.

reviews.title: Why do I keep trying
reviews.text:
Skechers makes shoes I find visually appealing. They tend to be very comfy right off the bat and they make lots of models in wide sizes. However, their quality is just terrible. I bought these in June for the new school year, but ended up wearing them starting in July because my previous pair of skechers was falling apart. Now only 2 months later, the inside heal of one shoe is wearing out, the loop on the back has already snapped and the memory foam has lost its omph.

I guess I but them because the prices are cheap, but not really. They used to be cheap, but when I need to replace them in less than a year, when I used to get 2 years out of them, there's a problem and they become expensive.
I'm an adult who mainly used these to walk to work. I don't beat on them. My Mizuno running shoes have a YEAR warranty. Skechers doesn't seem to have any warranty that I can see leaving this 15+ year customer saying, don't buy them and now I need to find another shoe brand that suits me.

reviews.title: This may be the shoe I have been looking for?
reviews.text:
I have planters Faseiitis and have been wearing the same style of Kmart shoes with orthotics for 8 years. Kmart is no longer making the shoe I was wearing. So, I have been looking for a replacement for several months, with no success. I have tried at least 10 different shoes in the 25-40 dollar price range with no success. My feet seem so sensitive to new shoes that the second I put them on my feet begin to hurt. Since I was having no success, I decided to spend a little more money and give the New Balance entry level shoe a try. When I first put them on they felt better than all the others I had tried. There is plenty of room in the 9W I ordered. Lots of padding. So with room and padding I was off to a good start. I had no problem removing the insoles and inserting my orthotics. As has been mentioned in other reviews, the straps are too short. One shoe was worse than the other, it would just barely close. This problem is resolving itself the more I wear the shoes. The other problem mentioned in other reviews is the fact that these shoes squeak. The noise is coming from the rubbing of the tonque on the Velcro straps. Not a deal breaker but annoying. I will try applying some saddle soap and see if that helps. The shoes appears to be well made with a very sturdy looking sole. I have been wearing the shoes now for a few hours each day. So far they are doing better than anything else I have tried. However, it is a little too early to know if this will be my new favorite shoe. In general, I think you should give this shoe a try if planters Faseiitis is a problem, or anyone looking for a good walking shoe at a fair price.
UPDATE-I used saddle soap on the tongue and straps and that stop the squeaking.
reviews.author: AGEGRCKAVOOM2RWMHNQZATHZTUXA

reviews.title: Saucony, what is going on?
reviews.text:
I used to LOVE Saucony shoes. Now I can't find one that fits. I've been a size 8.5 for decades, and Saucony 8.5 used to fit. I still have a pair of Saucony Stabil shoes that fit great (Too bad they don't make those anymore!)
Now I try size 8.5 and my toes are crammed to the end of the toebox. This is the second time this has happened, with two different styles. Maybe the next size up would fit, but who wants to ship back and forth looking for an appropriate replacement. Maybe Saucony needs to re-size their shoes back to their original sizing scheme. Ugh. At least there are free returns.

title: ASICS Gel-Quantum 360 Knit 2 Women's Running Shoe
description: Run hard, land soft. The GEL-QUANTUM 360 Knit shoe is constructed with 360 degrees of our GEL BRAND technology cushioning, balancing high performance and maximum comfort. A new stretch knit upper surrounds the foot in a custom, sock-like fit elevating this GEL-QUANTUM shoe to another level - helping you power through running and cross-training workouts. Weight: 10.4. Heel Height: 21. Forefoot Height: 11. Synthetic-and-mesh Ethylene Vinyl Acetate sole REARFOOT GEL BRAND TECHNOLOGY CUSHIONING SYSTEM: Attenuates shock during impact phase and allows for a smooth transition to midstance. HEEL CLUTCHING SYSTEM TECHNOLOGY: Exoskeletal heel counter provides improved support and creates improved heel fitting environment. FLUIDFIT UPPER: FLUIDFIT upper technology combines multi-directional stretch mesh with stretch reinforcements that adapt to the athlete's foot, creating a truly customized glove-like fit. ORTHOLITE LASTING: This lasting material features plush underfoot comfort while providing excellent moisture management and a high level of breathability (ORTHOLITE is a registered trademark of ATP Manufacturing LLC.) SOLYTE MIDSOLE MATERIAL: A lighter weight midsole compound than ASICS standard EVA and SpEVA midsoles. Also features enhanced cushioning and durability.

reviews.title: You Either Have Narrow Feet, or Numb Feet
reviews.text:
For the pros, I do LOVE the color (Birch, Feather Grey).
But, alike what others have stated before me, these would be a better choice for more narrow feet. I can’t run more than 3 miles on these without feeling stress on my legs, in addition to my feet going numb. It was my mistake that I assumed if they were described as running shoes (on Amazon), that they would factually be running shoes. These are not, not for long distance at least.
If I meant to buy something for the gym or leisure walking, they wouldn’t have been a problem.

title: New Balance Men's 410 V6 Trail Running Shoe
description: Be off-road ready in the New Balance 410v6 men’s trail shoe. Featuring a durable AT Tread rubber outsole, this shoe is fit for any adventure. Made with a sleek upper, ACTEVA midsole technology and a cushioned NB Comfort Insert, the versatile men’s running shoe is designed to keep you comfortable and stylish before, during and after your hike. New Balance, is dedicated to helping athletes achieve their goals. It's been their mission for more than a century. It's why they don't spend money on celebrity endorsements. They spend it on research and development. It's why they don't design products to fit an image. They design them to fit. New Balance is driven to make the finest shoes for the same reason athletes lace them up: to achieve the very best. 50% Synthetic, 50% Mesh Imported Rubber sole ACTEVA midsole cushioning delivers versatile, flexible support AT Tread outsole provides versatile traction during both on and off-road activities Lightweight injection-molded EVA foam midsole provides lightweight cushioning NB Comfort Insert offers additional cushioning Synthetic/mesh upper

reviews.title: Highs and Lows
reviews.text: These are only $40, and being made in the USA is a plus. I am using them for inside shoes (i.e. house shoes), so I'm not expecting a lot. The soles were stiff at first but have softened up to just the right bounce after a couple of weeks (I weigh 230 lbs). I bought a 12, and was able to insert a Dr Scholl's insert without cutting it. Those insoles are cut for a 13, though it is more like a 12.5. I wear 12/13- depending upon the shoe, and these feel just right at 12. If you are a wide foot then you might feel differently, of course. The two shortcomings are the 47 in laces (too short) and the lack of protection provided by the tongue. Even with elastic laces set up to be slip-ons, there is still an intrusion of pain. It may just be me and my sensitive feet, but I don't feel this way in a pair of Hokas (though they are 3 times the price. I already wear a pretty thick cotton sock, but the laces still transmit through to my foot. I may glue so moleskin to the underside of the tongue to deflect any "point loads" away from my wimpy feet.

reviews.title: Pretty Shoe But Memory Foam Cushioning Is Not Adequate for "Power" Walking
reviews.text:
This RYKA WOMEN'S SKY WALKING SHOE (Frost Grey/Aqua SKY/Iron Grey) is very pretty, and feels great when you put it on. However, it's not a good shoe for "power" (exercise) walking. The memory foam doesn't provide much impact cushioning, so the shoe does little to reduce the strain on my knees and hips that results from walking on the sloped concrete sidewalks around my neighborhood. I do like the sole very much, because it provides great traction when the sidewalks are wet or covered with seeds from overhanging trees.

I haven't used these shoes a lot, but my very first impression was that they were NOT well made, given the price (about $43). I believe the reviews that say the memory foam breaks down and that the shoes fall apart after a couple of months of serious exercise walking. The fit isn't bad for me--an 8B always seems to fit, in any shoe--as long as the laces are tight. If I fail to lace the shoes carefully, my feet slide forward and my toes bang against the ends when I walk. So maybe the shoes are a bit shorter than I expected, or maybe I don't need the narrow heel.

The exercise walking shoe that works for me is the [[ASIN:B00GXEFBYM ASICS Women's Gel Tech Neo 4 Walking Shoe]]. The ASICS shoe costs almost twice as much, but the gel cushioning takes a lot of strain off my knees and hips. I rate this RYKA Women's Sky Walking Shoe at a generous 3 stars ("It's okay" on the official Amazon scale).
reviews.author: AEYGPUCRKH7G4VM22FM3VAKSQ23Q

reviews.title: Very comfortable shoe if you don't have flat feet!
reviews.text:
The amount of comfort in this shoe is amazing. It competes easily with $130 overpronation shoes I own aside from no arch support. That's where it failed completely for me. Already tried replacing the insoles with arch support ones but the comfort level wasn't the same sadly. The provided insole is removable but is tacked down with adhesive. Didn't damage the shoes when removing it and with care I was able to put it back in.

So in conclusion, yes these are amazing shoes if you need some overpronation support or if your gait is neutral. In this case I'd highly recommend but sadly it didn't work for me. The shoes look amazing and they feel amazing. The price with the construction of the shoes is an amazing value if you just need some extra support and cushion. I'd consider this shoe to be plush in the midsole and the insole was comfortable but not a lot to make it heavy. If it weren't for my arch I would of ordered another shoe to keep in storage when this one wears out. Great service as usual from Amazon for the free return.

reviews.title: Very Pleased, watch your sizing
reviews.text: Based on other reviews I ordered 1/2 size up and W just to make sure!  Whew! I am glad I did because they are literally just right sockless.  Very comfortable, good look, good price.  I had Achilles tendon surgery in Feb this year and just now can wear shoes that are not clogs or mules.  Happy to have the support.  I wear a solid size ten and based on Keen, Propet, Uggs, Steins, Birkenstock, this has always been a good size.  I never could find an Asics to fit years ago at my old Shoe Station, but I also had no choice for a Wide or 10.5.  Eleven is just too long.  My big toe is not jammed up against the toe box AND I can actually lace up and tie the sneaker with less than 3" across the lacing area.  Very happy.  Just watch your size.

description: Reebok is an inspirational athletic lifestyle company that has always lived at the intersection of sport and style.​ Their fitness heritage gives them credibility in the sports space while their foundation in Classics gives them a foothold in lifestyle.​ Reebok makes products that inspire human movement—and they advocate for human rights for all. Reebok is guided by the fundamental belief that LIFE IS NOT A SPECTATOR SPORT. ​No matter who you are, where you came from, how you move, or what you look like –​ everyone deserves to move freely. 100% Soft Leather Imported Rubber sole Shaft measures approximately low-top" from arch Adult Tennis Shoes: Inspired by the 1988 HRN World Tour merch, these tennis shoes for men and women feature a signature tongue label as a reminder that the time for human rights is always right now Premium Construction: These casual sneakers come equipped with a leather upper complete with a signature design celebrating the return of the Reebok Human Rights Award Efficient Foot Support: These casual shoes feature lightweight cushioning and a padded foam sock liner that keeps you moving comfortably all day long Superior Performance: Ideal for daily and athleisure wear, these casual shoes for men and women features rubber die-cut EVA midsole and outsole for traction Signature Style: Black sneakers with a bold contrast-color outsole and a tongue label inspired by the iconic 1988 HRN World Tour merch are a symbol of the change the world still needs to see

title: Skechers Sport Women's Premium-Premix Slip-On Sneaker
description: Free yourself from the hassle of laces. The Premix is a great-looking, classic sneaker in an easy-to-wear, slip-on style. Elastic insets ensure a snug yet comfortable fit as well as an easy on and off. Additional comfort features include a padded tongue and collar, a cushioned footbed, and an EVA midsole for great shock absorption. A durable non-marking rubber outsole delivers excellent grip. Skechers is an award-winning global leader in the lifestyle footwear industry that designs, develops, and markets lifestyle footwear that appeals to trend-savvy men, women and children. The company's success stems from it's high quality, diversified, and affordable product line that meets consumers' various lifestyle needs. Since it's inception in 1992, the Skechers diverse product offering has grown from utility style boots to include seven Skechers brands and five uniquely branded fashion lines for men and women. 100% Leather and synthetic Rubber sole Slip-on sneaker with polished hardware and cushioned tongue and collar Supportive sole Padded collar and tongue

reviews.title: Cute, easy to walk in outside. Top ankle area is tight and rigid
reviews.text:
I just bought a pair of the Sketchers knit shoes to wear around the house and I like them so much that I tried these for outdoor use. The shoe itself is not bad looking and has a reasonable amount of arch support built in.

My issue is with the top, around the top of the foot and ankle. It's just tight and inflexible. After a week of wearing these, that top is still very tight - it hasn't really broken in or loosened up. I like that these are slip on, but there still should be some give in the tongue area.

title: Rockport Men's Eureka Walking Shoe Sneaker
description: The Ridgefield Eureka combines the comfort of a walking shoe with the look of an everyday casual-so you never have to compromise. With San Crispino construction for heel stability, generous collar and tongue padding, removable footbed, and a Strobel construction build for forefoot flexibility. Rockport has always been one step ahead of the times, believing the perfect shoe has not yet been invented. This positive dissatisfaction compels them to constantly improve upon their footwear. Founded in 1971, they were the first company to use advanced athletic technologies in casual shoes to achieve lightweight comfort, and the first company to engineer walking comfort in dress and casual shoes. 100% Leather Imported Rubber sole Shaft measures approximately low-top from arch GENUINE LEATHER: Leather upper is easy to clean and maintain, so these walking shoes for men look and feel even better with time FATIGUE-FIGHTING FOOTBED: Latex foam footbed generously cushions the foot to help reduce foot fatigue as you stand and walk BREATHABLE LINING: Moisture-wicking mesh lining offers breathability to create extra-comfortable walking shoes for men IMPACT-ABSORBING: EVA midsole provides flexible, lightweight impact absorption STABILITY AND TRACTION: Rubber outsole offers long-wearing durability, and helps to maintain a sure stride on a variety of surfaces A STABLE STRIDE: San Crispino construction provides a wide base of support for all-day stability EXTRA PADDING: Padded collar helps to prevent blisters and irritation for optimal cushioned comfort

reviews.title: Rockports are great shoes!!
reviews.text: Ordered for my son for his graduation from high school. They look good, the fit was comfortable over all--with one exception. Something about the 'tongue' where it hit the top of his foot annoyed him. Made his foot sore. He loosened the shoes, still did not work .  He made it through being on stage, the reception and going out after. I told him we will pad the shoe to see if it helps, or go to a shoe repair for assistance. I do recommend these shoes: they are Rockports, which my husband has worn for years. The shoes will wear like iron, they have good support, my son can wear these to work. They are worth the little bit of money I will invest in them.

title: Rockport Men's World Tour Classic Walking Shoe Sneaker
description: The World Tour is a Rockport legend. With its relaxed fit and Rockport Walking Platform construction, the men's World Tour walking shoe delivers what it takes to go the extra mile. Additional comfort features include a padded tongue and collar. In 1971, founded on the idea that style and comfort are two sides of the same coin, Rockport was the first to introduce dress shoes infused with sport technology. Today, building on generations of shoemaking experience, Rockport continues to deliver extraordinary technology-based comfort, incorporating the latest advances in construction and design into both modern and classic dress shoes, casual shoes, boots, sandals and everywhere in between. You'll breeze through the day in comfort and style with the M7100 Pro Walker from Rockport Men's. This tried and true classic sports a textured leather upper with a rounded toe, along with padded tongue and collar for maximum comfort. Cushioned insole cradles your foot, and the lace-up design allows for a perfect fit. Rockport has always been one step ahead of the times, believing the perfect shoe has not yet been invented. This positive dissatisfaction compels them to constantly improve upon their footwear. Founded in 1971, they were the first company to use advanced athletic technologies in casual shoes to achieve lightweight comfort, and the first company to engineer walking comfort in dress and casual shoes. See more 100% Leather Imported Rubber sole Shaft measures approximately low-top from arch Full grain leather upper EVA midsole Rubber outsole Fabric K70885, K71181, K70884

reviews.title: Something happen in this last year.
reviews.text: I have been  buying Rockports for 18 years. Started out with the ProWalkers and do to the quality dimensioning I started getting World Tour Classic Walking in January 2016. Just bought the pair for this year March 1st. Something happen within the last year with quality. Like someone else said the tongue of the shoe is now too short. I haven't notice any issues with stitching my issues are my sore feet trying to break these in. I can only wear these 2 to 3 times a week before I have to switch back to my old pair. This has never happen with the Rockports and its very upsetting. I do get Platar Fasciitis so the first thing I do is put in good arch supports. So is it my arch supports that aren't doing the job which is what I thought first. When I switched back to my old pair of World Touring Classic from last year I switched the Arch support too. After a few days my feet got better. Switched back to my new pair with arch support and I am sitting here typing this dreading getting up to work because the bottom of my feet hurt.

reviews.title: Very comfortable & supportive
reviews.text: I stand for 10+ hours a day without sitting even once. Mix in speed walking a couple miles & by the end of the day my arthritic knees, feet, & back are done. I needed new shoes for work & bought these because they were $100 less than my last pair, I liked the color selection, & figured if they didn't suit working in them well I could use them as everyday sneakers. I noticed a difference the moment I put them on. The shock absorption is fantastic & really gives great relief to my knees. I worked double shifts 2 days in a row & can't imagine how I would have felt had I not had these on. I did add inserts for my arches though because mine are higher than average & I had an extra pair. I wouldn't say the inserts are a necessity but if you have a problematic area getting some specifically for that will make these even better. I will definitely be ordering more of these for when I wear out the pair I have & possibly some for everyday use.

reviews.title: Finally,  a sneaker that helps, not hurts!
reviews.text: So I have horrible feet.  Bunions, hammer toe, flat feet etc... I am on my feet constantly for my job and I would come home to slowly die my feet were hurting so bad.  I've tried all kinds of sneakers and thought the 'gravity defyer' ones were great at first for walking around... but not at the job.  Plus the toe box wasn't large enough.  On a last ditch effort I decided to try the sketchers max cusion... omg...I wear them every night at work and now my feet aren't my problem.  I did get a size 9 even though I'm normally an 8 and a half (because of the hammer toe). They are perfect.  Not too big at all. They're light weight and feel like little clouds.  I'm ordering another pair just to have and getting rid of a few of my others.

title: ASICS Gel-Excite 4 Men's Running Shoe
description: For moderate mileage runners, the GEL-Excite 4 shoe offers comfort and durability. Our signature Rearfoot and forefoot gel cushioning technology softens brisk impact, while our ahar outsole targets high-impact zones for increased resistance wear and tear. In 1949, Mr. Kihachiro Onitsuka began his athletic footwear company (Onitsuka Co., Ltd.) by manufacturing basketball shoes out of his living room in Kobe, Japan. He chose the name ASICS for his company in 1977, based on a famous Latin phrase "Anima Sana In Corpore Sano," which, when translated, expresses the ancient ideal of "A Sound Mind in a Sound Body." Taking the acronym of this phrase, ASICS was founded on the belief that the best way to create a healthy and happy lifestyle is to promote total health and fitness. Today, ASICS offers a full line of performance-driven athletic shoes and technical active sports apparel and accessories dedicated to bringing harmony to the body and soul. 100% Synthetic Imported Rubber sole Rearfoot GEL Cushioning System: Attenuates shock during impact phase and allows for a smooth transition to midstance. Removable Sockliner: A sockliner which can be removed to accommodate a medical orthotic. AHAR Outsole: Acronym for ASICS High Abrasion Rubber. Placed in critical areas of the outsole for exceptional durability. Reflectivity

reviews.title: Bought these for fall/winter wear.
reviews.text: I have worn sketchers slip on sneakers for many years.  This pair  I just purchased, contains memory foam & is different in fit and style  from the D'lites slip on mule that I usually purchase. This resilient slip on is quite comfortable, and fits very well but it differs in so far  as I am able to "walk out" of the shoes frequently.  I am unable to ascertain at this point what the difference is, or why this happens, but I am tending to think that the way the shoe is cut & shaped in the upper heel  area, allows a little more width, where in my foot is able to slide out of the shoe much easier.  I didn't return the sneakers because I needed a black pair for winter. But I am able to cope with the negativity of walking out of the shoes because in bad weather I will be wearing a heavier boot. I will not probably buy this particular style and type again. I just hope that sketchers  continues to manufacture the D lights slip on's that I normally buy.

reviews.title: Comfortable, Good Traction & They Look Good!
reviews.text:
My husband usually wears a 10.5 and occasionally an 11. His toes touched the edge of these shoes, but he has thick socks on he said. So, these run true to size or a tad small. They don't run "somewhat large" according to his feet.

He finds these shoes comfortable. He has been wearing them all day.

He really likes the traction and the flexibility he said.

I like the appearance of these shoes. The medium gray is neutral and looks good with jeans, gray slacks, dark slacks etc. It's a great, understated shoe. He doesn't run, but he does a lot of physical labor so these should be comfortable for that.

He is very happy with these shoes. I'll report back if there's any change.

Initially I didn't want to use this pair to go to the park, walk dogs or on a messy rain day. But...they are so comfortable that eventually you just forget to change shoes. They become worn and older looking. So now, I have my Yard Pair for everyday yard work, dog walks and just around the house and errands. And I also have a nice pair I can keep clean and neat. New balance makes some of the best running shoes (in my opinion) and these Roav's did not disappoint. I will be buying additional pairs in other colors.

reviews.title: Good basic shoe but not long lasting if you are on your feet a lot
reviews.text: I purchased these shoes for support at work but was wary as I wore out the support in a new pair of NB new shoes in the past during a week of set-up and weekend of holding a garage sale.  These lasted about 2 weeks before the support wore down and my feet started hurting at the end of the day.  I am in the medical field and treat patients all day but I am not constantly on my feet like a nurse.  These would be good for casual wear and walks but not for durability.  One thing that I did not like initially (which is an easy fix with replacement laces) is that the corded laces come undone easily and I am having to retie these 3-4 times a day (even though I double knot them).

reviews.title: Awesome comfort for difficult feet
reviews.text: Purchased these for my husband to wear when are traveling.  He is not comfortable in flip flops or other sandals and going through various securities, it is nice to have a shoe that is easy on/ easy off - even when you are TSA pre-check.  These fit the bill.  My husband is diabetic and has sensitive, wide feet with a couple of hammer toes. He basically uncomfortable in most shoes. Very challenging.  These are the first shoes we have purchased he has worn for hours and hours and not complained once.  I will say that while these Sketchers are the ultimate in comfort, they (both women's and men's) break down within 6 months or so with fairly regular wear, so we keep a few pairs on hand and recycle the old ones.  The older we get, the more important comfort is and these really work.  I do recommend that if you have not tried a pair on in person, it is a good idea to do so.  Each style and sometimes different fabrics fit a little differently I've found.  But once you have the general idea, you can order them fairly reliably online.  I always update if there are any wear or other issues that may be helpful.
reviews.author: AFZUK3MTBIBEDQOPAK3OATUOUKLA

title: New Balance Women's Fresh Foam 1080 V10 Running Shoe
description: Give your running gear a stylish update with the New Balance Fresh Foam 1080 v10. This performance running shoe delivers a sleek and impressive design that's packed with data-driven comfort. Part of the Fresh Foam X collection and made with our signature Fresh Foam cushioning, this responsive runner is made to encourage you mile after mile as you conquer short training runs, long distances and everything in between. These running shoes are our premium expression of runner’s data, supreme comfort, and cutting-edge design, all of which you'll appreciate as you lace them up ahead of a run. Experience 360-degrees of comfort in these fitness running shoes, thanks to the Ultra Heel technology and engineered knit upper. You'll love the data-driven design that features a strong and durable blown rubber outsole, a Hypoknit upper with plenty of stretch and support, and an Ortholite sockliner for underfoot comfort. Select styles of the Fresh Foam 1080 v10 may be eligible for Medicare reimbursement with Medicare/HCPCS code A5500. 50% Synthetic, 50% Mesh Imported Rubber sole Cushioned Running Shoes for Every Runner: Part of the Fresh Foam X collection, these shoes use our latest advancements in data to design to deliver precise plushness exactly where you need it Support and Durability: This shoe features a Hypoknit upper designed to provide strategic areas of stretch and support, in addition to a blown rubber outsole for impressive durability Perfect Fit: These comfortable running shoes include a bootie upper construction and an Ultra Heel design, both of which hug the back of the foot for a snug, supportive fit Breathable and Lightweight Running Shoes: The supportive engineered knit upper includes synthetic and mesh materials for amazing breathability during runs of any distance Keep Your Feet Comfortable: The Ortholite sockliner in these performance running shoes helps keep your feet feeling fresh as you power through your toughest runs

reviews.title: Tight across the top of the foot - OUCH!
reviews.text:
So on my quest for comfortable shoes with both support and cushion, I ordered these sneakers. I'm not a runner (I walk), so I was a little hesitant to spend quite this much on shoes, but I was striking out all over the place on finding shoes.

So - the good first, these shoes are REALLY cushiony (is that a word?). They feel nice and soft like walking on springy clouds. The shoes are attractive and lightweight.

The bad - the portion that is over the top of your foot, where the laces start, is so tight! I ordered a wide shoe and it was fine except for that fit, It squeezed the top of my foot terribly. I figured that they could be broken in and it would loosen up, but for that price tag, I want the shoes to fit right out of the box. From other reviews, I don't think this is due to my wide foot. If you have narrow/skinny feet or don't mind taking the time to break these in, they do have great cushioning.

reviews.title: Best shoes for neuropathy pain EVER!!
