title: New Balance Men's 877 V1 Walking Shoe
description: The New Balance 877 walking shoe is designed to help you move in comfort. Featuring the same sole as the iconic 577 model from New Balance, this stylish walking shoe for adults delivers premium comfort and support with a fresh new look. Equipped with Walking Strike Path technology in the outsole, these cushioned shoes promote a proper stride by helping to stabilize and guide your feet trough their natural walking gait cycle. Other technological features include ABZORB midsole technology that combines cushioning and compression resistance to help minimize the damage from impacts as you log your miles. A durable rubber outsole stands up well to everyday wear-and-tear so you can keep moving with confidence. While the 877 does a great job of protecting and supporting your feet, it’s no slouch in the style department either. These comfortable walking shoes have a sleek athletic look inspired by New Balance heritage designs. The suede and mesh upper pops with classic New Balance branding and reflective details that are designed to catch the light – and the attention of your walking buddies. These shoes were proudly assembled in New Balance's U.S. factories – since the domestic value is less than 70%, we have labeled them "Assembled in the USA." Select styles of the 877 may be eligible for Medicare reimbursement with Medicare/HCPCS code A5500. 100% Suede/mesh Imported Rubber sole 11 mm drop: due to variances created during the development and manufacturing processes, all references to 11 mm drop are approximate Medicare / HCPCS code = A5500, may be eligible for Medicare reimbursement Rubber outsole Suede/mesh upper These shoes were proudly assembled in our U.S. factories by our more than 1,200 U.S. workers. Since the domestic value is less than 70%, we have labeled them "Assembled in the USA."

With a range of colors and details, and available for both men and women, the 574v2's possibilities are endless.Step up your street style with this iconic sneaker. Featuring the signature silhouette sneakerheads have loved since the '80s, these iconic kicks are a must-have in your shoe closet. High-Tech: These aren't your typical lifestyle shoes. They have built-in technology for long-term wearability. ENCAP midsole cushioning combines soft foam with a durable polyurethane rim to deliver all-day support. title: Salomon Men's Speedcross 4 Trail Running Shoes
description: The 4Th edition of our iconic and aggressively lugged trail runner for tearing through technical, soft ground with speed. Salomon was born in 1947 in the heart of the French Alps and the birthplace of modern alpinism. Driven by a passion for skiing and design innovation, Francois Salomon and his son George designed and perfected much of the first modern ski equipment. During the following 60 years, Salomon's commitment to innovative design and passion for mountain sports created a vast range of revolutionary new concepts in boots, skis and apparel and brought innovative solutions to footwear, apparel and equipment for hiking, adventure racing, mountaineering and trail running. Through performance-driven design, Salomon enhances mountain sports by converting new ideas into action and expanding the limits of possibility. 100% Synthetic Imported Rubber sole Shaft measures approximately low-top from arch The fourth generation of our legendary Speedcross lug pattern is an even hungrier monster for eating up soft, technical trails Feel one with the trail with a precise combination of Sensifit with Quicklace, performance last shape and endoFit Just enough to provide protection for technical trail running Salomon 2 year limited warranty

reviews.title: The Solomon product is by far the best shoe or trail running and hikin
reviews.text:
Solomon shoes. I'm on my third or fourth pair I think. I had the original speed cross and then I had speed cross two I think. Now I have to speak Cross 4 they have made minor improvements. The shoe is rock solid. I have a pair of Solomon speed treks same but don't fit as good as the speed cross. At least for my foot.

The speed cross have amazing grip on trail. I never wear them on concrete. I put them on before I hit the trail and take them off as soon as I get back to the car. The only downside is they're not the greatest on slick Rock or You still have to be a little careful when running or walking on slab rock. I hike in rain, cross streams or rivers. I hike up rivers. I don't take my shoes off for any obstacle. I'd wear Gators the Solomon version that fit perfect on the shoe. The drive super fast on trail unless it's cold out. I wear the compression Solomon socks with them. That trio is a perfect set for long strenuous sites through any condition.

I am forever sold on Solomon products shoes and everything else.
reviews.author: AG3FBN5AFOE6DD6WQRQYNUOINLBA

reviews.title: Sized way too small, at least for less than perfect feet
reviews.text:
I've been buying Salomon for years, this was the first time I had to return a pair of their shoes.
I could barely put my feet in them, and when I did they hurt, so I sent them back and shall find another style.

I hope this isn't the trend, but these fit like Nikes - so if you have perfect feet then by all means buy these, otherwise stay away because they were too narrow and too tight for me.

Quality is superior for those still interested - they are so well made.

reviews.title: This purchase was difficult for me
reviews.text: This was a more difficult purchase for me that I thought.  I only wanted to purchase a pair of sneakers for walking around but as I am an old man now, I was most conscious of a non skid type of sole.  I looked all thought the Amazon web pages but as I was looking for pictures and descriptions of the soles I had a difficult time because the web pages do not show the soles.  Then went to a high profile name brand brick and mortar store but this store didn't have what I wanted anyway so I went back to Amazon and purchased this pair of Reeboks.  On this particular web page there was just enough of the tip of the sole showing to give me a good idea of the sole and as it was acceptable to me, I then made my purchase and it appears to be very satisfactory.

reviews.title: Classic look at affordable price
reviews.text:
I'm a football official and these are a nice compromise to all the options out there. The price is very nice, but they are comfortable, lightweight and well built. I have had issues with NB not bending at the right place for my foot. That has caused toe discomfort and even cramping. These do not to this. title: Brooks Women's Ghost 14 Neutral Running Shoe
description: Imported Rubber sole THIS WOMEN'S SHOE IS FOR: Runners looking for a smooth ride that won’t distract from the fun of the run. The Ghost 14 offers a simplified midsole construction which creates more seamless transitions. This Brooks Ghost 14 is a certified PDAC A5500 Diabetic shoe and has been granted the APMA Seal of Acceptance. SUPPORT AND CUSHION: The Ghost 14 offers neutral support while providing high energizing cushioning. Ideal for road running, cross training, the gym or wherever you might want to take them! Predecessor: Ghost 13 BALANCED, SOFT CUSHIONING: BioMoGo DNA and DNA LOFT cushioning work together to provide a just-right softness underfoot without losing responsiveness and durability - yet it feels lighter than ever. DNA LOFT now makes up the entire midsole, for a softer, smoother, ride. SMOOTH, STABLE RIDE: No matter how your foot lands, our Segmented Crash Pad - an integrated system of shock absorbers - will cushion every step and stride for smooth heel-to-toe transitions. SOFT, SECURE, FIT: More strategic use of the 3D Fit Print upper technology provides structure that moves with the foot.

reviews.title: Beware of imposters
reviews.text:

reviews.text:
I have no idea what this company was thinking about, but the model I am reviewing was absolutely awful for me.
I have been thrilled with my Brooks ghost 11 shoes, but they keep “improving” the design.
After a 20 minute brisk walk my hip hurt in a way it has never hurt before. It was intense and uncomfortable. Luckily, it only lasted for a day.
There was no way I was going to wear these a second time.
I went to my local running store and found the Brooks glycerin 19.
They have them on Amazon, too. Just remarkably comfortable. Great cushioning and support.
I am not a fan of company’s changing a fabulous product. Innovation is not always improvement.

reviews.title: great so far
reviews.text: Bought these for my husband to replace the old shoes he wore to death and so far, they are great. The shoes look nice and are very soft and comfortable. They fit as expected and bend when you walk naturally, following the curves of the foot so there isn't a lot of pain involved when getting used to them. reviews.title: Smallish fit, 4E = 2E, and tight in the length (go a half-size up). Also, this is a SUPPORT shoe...
reviews.text: Unfortunately, they do not make this in a 2E, only the 4E. Interestingly, this is not really a true 4E, more like a 3E--as is typical of Nike products, this is narrower than advertised. I need a true 2E from Nike, and a regular D/M width in any other manufacturer (Brooks, Asics, NB). Also, these are a little short for the size. Recommend a half-size up. Note that although this is not indicated anywhere in the product listing, this is a SUPPORT shoe for people who pronate. I supinate slightly (the opposite), so this is not the right shoe for me. Solid shoes though--this is one of the most durable shoes I have ever owned--extremely durable, in fact. You will go through lots of laces before these are worn out. I wore mine for years until I finally got a proper education in supportive footwear. Turns out these are totally the wrong shoe for me and were screwing up my ankles and knees because they increased my supination (rolling of the foot outward), instead of preventing it. I need a "neutral" shoe, and these are a "support" shoe.
reviews.author: AGQCFT47KDI77JKRSOMAK34MJPAQ

reviews.title: These are so comfortable. Great cushioning
reviews.text: These are so comfortable. Great cushioning, perfect fit. When I wear other shoes, I wish I was back in these, so I started wearing these a lot. Bought the grey ones in May, and bought white ones in July, and I alternate wearing them back and forth. They wash easily, just leave them out and dry in a day or two, and still look new, and it's now late February. Just curious, I looked them up on Amazon to see if they are still available for the day when I'll replace them (but not for awhile yet), and there were two left in my size for each color. So I just ordered one of each. Two shoes for $100 for excellent shoes I love, that's pretty great! I'll save these new ones until my current two are ready to replace.

description: Known for durability, stability, and its rugged style, the GEL-Kahana 8 shoe offers our GEL Cushioning System for comfort and performance where the rubber meets the road or trail. SpEVA Midsole: Material Improves bounce-back characteristics and decreases midsole breakdown. DuoMax Support System: A dual-density midsole system positioned to enhance support and stability. Trusstic System: Reduces the weight of the sole unit while retaining the structural integrity of the shoe. Trail Specific Outsole: Reversed lugs provide uphill and downhill traction on all types of terrain. Fit Tip: If between sizes, order 0.5 size up ; Weight: 11.9 oz ; ASICS GEL-Kahana 8 is made for neutral- Foot Type:Normal size arches , Over- Foot Type :Low arches or flat feet.

reviews.title: 4th kahana.  Walk-around shoe.
reviews.text:
I don't run anymore. These are walk about town shoes. This is my 4th pair of kahanas. Go back to 5th it was trabuco.
Try kahana vs venture in a store. You'll notice the difference.
Just my walkabout pair.
Regrets?
Saucony marauder. Mizuno wave legend. NB 626.
You can fix those with scholls.
You don't have to fix these.
Lace them loose or go up a size and lace tight.
Again, not trail running in these. Simple daily about town...you won't find a better more comfortable shoe for the price. PERIOD.
If these don't do it for you go get the Mizuno wave rider. Or gt2000.
What's amusing is I have no clue when I bought my previous kahana 8s.
And I just bought another kahana 8.
Again,if you can find a better pair of walkabouts, let me know...for the price.
You won't. This is it.
Toebox has definitely narrowed from previous versions... notice the lacing. 12s still a 12.

reviews.title: WAY too tight over the foot.  NOT for Diabetics, IMHO!
reviews.text:
I've had these on for about an hour now, so I am sure they are not returnable. That's too bad, because an hour has shown me that they are very uncomfortable. I cannot imagine who would find them wearable. Maybe someone with abnormally narrow feet.

The VERY stretchy material they are made from makes them hard to get on. You MUST use a shoehorn, and even then, it is not easy. They also - unpleasantly - squeeze the foot. After an hour, my toes are getting cold from low circulation! Seriously! Plus, the material does not breathe well, despite it's "perforated" appearance. So my feet are hot and wet.

The laces are just decoration. It's the tight material that holds them on. Plus, the artsy-craftsy arch inset in the instep is an invitation to soiling underneath it, without giving much arch support.
Could these be knock-offs? I assumed the attractive price was because they are a discontinued color. Maybe they are just fakes!.

PS THESE ARE NOT FOR DIABETICS. There is too much restriction of circulation.

I was advised to get "caged" Ultraboosts...the ones that lace normally. (The "cage" is the plastic framework for the laces.) But I liked the color of these.

Big mistake.

reviews.title: Selling Out Fast!
reviews.text:
I hadn’t ran in an official race for many years - so I haven’t had legit long distance running shoes since. I bought the size 7’s since I saw they were labelled as the last pair and that’s the size I’ve been wearing for the ASICS Quantum line. Once the Nimbus 22’s were delivered, I realized that I can wear these as regular shoes, but not running shoes since I need the extra space... such a shame, I can’t find 7.5’s anywhere in this same color.

Besides this, I was surprised how cushioned these felt when I first tried them on. I have wide feet and these didn’t seem too narrow.

Update:

I tried the Nimbus 22’s in both size 7 and 7.5 and they are still too small... the difference in the 7.5 is that it is wider, but not so much taller.. I was certain the half size would be enough but it doesn’t seem so, my toes barely graze the top of the shoe in the 7.5’s.

To reiterate, I wear the Quantum 360 v5’s in ***size 7*** with a thumb width’s worth at the top of the toe box. This model is taller and more narrow of a shoe than the Nimbus 22’s. It looks like I will have to try the Nimbus’s in a full size bigger than the Quantum’s, hopefully the size 8’s would be it for me.

Online shopping can be a bit frustrating but thank goodness for the return policy.

Pictured from left to right:

Quantum 360 5
GT-2000 8
Nimbus 22
reviews.author: AFXMRCGTLJ3SVDUE6QZOCHURCFCA

reviews.title: Finally back to the wider toe box
reviews.text: The Nimbus has been my go-to since 2013 and I have enjoyed all of them. The 18's went to a narrower toebox and generally narrow overall, which was fine, but did cause me some discomfort while breaking them in. The 22's feel much more like the first Nimbus's that I got years ago and I am so happy with them. I had no pain on the outside edges of my feet during my first wear, like I did with the 18's. It is interesting that these are "gender specific", as in they have a narrow scoop around the upper heel/lower ankle to keep them on the foot and supposedly something to do with the "trussic" for female gait. Not sure about that part, but my socks didn't slip down this time, and I felt great after my run/walk. I recommend these shoes. Comfy, padded and stable.

title: Brooks Women's Adrenaline GTS 22 Supportive Running Shoe
description: Smooth and steady wins the run: our most-loved support shoe is better than ever. With an updated midsole made with 100% DNA LOFT, and featuring our trusted GuideRails support, the Adrenaline GTS 22 delivers our smoothest ride yet. Imported THIS WOMEN'S SHOE IS FOR: Runners looking for a smooth ride, with great cushion and trusted GuideRails support that won’t distract from the fun of the run. This Brooks Adrenaline GTS 22 is a certified PDAC A5500 Diabetic shoe and has been granted the APMA Seal of Acceptance. We recommend ordering Brooks running shoes 1/2 to one size larger than your dress shoe. SUPPORT AND CUSHION: Provides just the right amount of stability and support, great for overpronation while providing high energizing cushioning. Ideal for road running, cross training, the gym or wherever you might want to take them! Predecessor: Adrenaline GTS 21 BALANCED, SOFT CUSHIONING: The updated midsole is now 100% DNA LOFT cushioning paired with the Segmented Crash Pad for an even softer, smoother ride and easier flow from landing to toe-off. MODERNIZED FIT: More strategic use of the 3D Fit Print upper technology offers the structure and proven fit the Adrenaline is known for without excess bulk. GUIDERAILS HOLISTIC SUPPORT SYSTEM: We've shifted our focus beyond the feet to the most injury-prone part of a runner’s body: the knees. GuideRails keep you moving comfortably by keeping excess movement in check.

reviews.title: Good but not very stylish
reviews.text: I bought this shoe because I have plantar fasciatis (sp?) and my doctor wanted me to get a good shoe with a solid sole. Not one that would bend in half. So I ordered this and I have to say that my problem is well on its way to being healed. However  I can't say much for the fashion, they're very wide at the ball of the foot and don't look very pretty in my estimation but I bought them for a specific reason and for that they're working out.

reviews.text: Brooks are my favorite!  I'm on my feet a lot either working out or walking they always give me the support I need and they are comfortable all day!  I am 5' 10", 175lbs & 57 years young! I ordered a 10.5 and they fit perfectly.  I stopped buying size 10 altogether in all brands I think my feet got bigger as I got older and even though I don't like it the 10.5 gives my toes a little more room and being on the go I'm more about comfort and support.  They are always stylish and these are light grey, pink and black! Highly recommend!

reviews.title: DO NOT BUY GTS 21 as replacement. Unwearable-painful. Ruined my feet on my 20 mile training run.
reviews.text: I have bought probably at least 14 pairs of Adrenalines in the older models over the past few years as a marathon runner. I had a 20 mile training run today and put on this new pair of GTS 22 (I had been running in the 21s but they are now discontinued). I almost couldn't finish my run-the toe box is so much narrower and less give, my toes were in excruciating pain after just a few miles. I also have bleeding blisters on my heels. I will never wear these again. Lost 130 dollars on a pair of shoes that is useless. DO NOT BUY AS REPLACEMENT FOR GTS 21. I wish I could get my money back. They are NOT THE SAME SHOE  as before. Terrible product.

reviews.title: After about 3 months these feel flat and my feet hurt from walking relatively short distances daily
reviews.text: They don’t serve me as long as I could hope.  For the price.  Maybe they need a better insole.  After 3 months, my back and neck and feet hurt.  I walk the neighborhood daily.  And would like to do modest jogging now that it’s spring.  Anyway.  This is my second or third pair of these recently.  I think maybe I liked them at first because of the slightly wider toe box than Nike Air Max.  I moved up from a size 8 and 1/2 to a size 9.  And now they don’t rub the heel to form blisters there.  My toes don’t feel cramped together tightly.  They have a very good feel at first, for a few months only, due to the cushioning.  There is not a lot of support - for the clunkiest of this shoe there’s only a moderate amount of support.  Yet some flexibility to run or walk easily, without feeling too heavy or clunky.  Nice look.  But I need a shoe at this price that feels great for more than 3 months only!  Wow!

reviews.title: Great shoes in the early going!
reviews.text: I read a few reviews of these, and a lot of folks said they were tight and had a long break-in period.  I just wanted to get a review up because my experience has been the opposite!  So far, I've put about 35 miles on them.  I did some cross training work outs in them (mainly boxing) just to break them in for a couple hours before running in them.  So far, so good!  I'm a 10.5 wide, and I have actually found the toe box to fit the same as my 21s.  They also don't ride up as high on my ankle in the back as the 21s, so I've avoided the nasty blisters the 21s would sometimes give me.  All in all, I'm loving these so far!  They're a good training shoe.  Supportive, maybe a little heavy but I can live with that for training.  So far so good!

title: OLUKAI Men's Sneaker
description: The easiest boat shoe. Made with no-tie laces and breathable materials that are perfect for life by the water. Translation: Handsome (noh-heh-ah moh-koo) Made to elevate the traditional boat shoe, we crafted the handsome Nohea Moku with an adventurous life in mind. Sure-footed and down for any outdoor exploration, the no-tie laces and stretch bootie construction for barefoot wear yield a shoe that’s ready for land or water. Rubber sole THE ULTIMATE ADVENTURE SHOE: Made to elevate the traditional boat shoe, we crafted the handsome Nohea Moku men’s shoe with an adventurous life in mind. Sure-footed and down for any outdoor exploration, the no-tie laces and stretch bootie construction for barefoot wear yield a shoe that’s ready for land or water. 2-IN-1 SHOE & SLIDE: The Nohea Moku features our patented Drop-In Heel design for both a shoe and slide functionality where the heel can be folded down for slip-on convenience, and a stretch bootie construction for barefoot wear. These no tie boat shoes feature a lightweight breathable mesh design with a stretch air mesh tongue and gusset, elastic lacing system for easy on/off use, medial water drainage ports and are minimally lined with a soft breathable microfiber. ALL-DAY COMFORT: The dual-density anatomical PU footbed with gel insert delivers sustained all-day comfort and cushioning, while the soft, quick-drying jersey knit or single mesh layer footbed cover allows for maximum breathability, and is removable and washable. LIGHTWEIGHT, DURABLE & STYLISH: Our Nohea Moku shoe features an all-weather, non-marking rubber cupsole with added traction on wet surfaces and non-slip safety. Available in mens shoe size 7 - 16. ONE YEAR WARRANTY: As a testament to our quality construction, all OluKai footwear is backed by a guarantee against manufacturer’s defects for one full year from the date of purchase, including defects such as frayed or loose stitching and straps pulling out where soles are not excessively worn. title: ASICS Gel-Nimbus 20 Women's Running Shoe
description: The Gel-Nimbus 20 shoe has a flexible vertical groove that decouples tools along the progression line to improve gait efficiency. The exoskeletal heel counterweight provides improved support and creates an improved heel fitment environment. It has a gel cushioning system for rear hind and forefoot, that reduces the impact during the detachment phases and allows the movement on several levels while the foot passes through the step cycle.The material of the speva midsole improves the characteristics of rebound and reduces midsole breakage. It has a new blown ahar outsole that offers advanced cushioning, durability and foot stroke, in addition it is built with the innovative midsole foam, flytefoam. Its organic fibers bounce metarun provide a perfect cushioning from the beginning of the stroke to the end.It is designed with a 3 m reflective material, for visibility. Textile and Synthetic Imported Rubber sole Shaft measures approximately low-top from arch FLYTEFOAM MIDSOLE TECHNOLOGY Our FlyteFoam technology provides exceptional bounce back and responsiveness no matter the distance, utilizing organic super fibers to help reduce packing out that traditionally happens with softer, low density foams. FLUIDRIDE MIDSOLE: ASICS FluidRide midsole provides the ultimate combination of bounce back and cushioning properties with reduced weight and exceptional durability.

reviews.title: I won't buy these anymore.
reviews.text: I have been wearing Asics Gel Nimbus 20s for quite a few years now; I think I got my first pair 5-7 years ago but I'm not sure. At any rate, the pair I bought last month will be my last pair, because these shoes are not what they once were. The company seems to have chosen cost savings over comfort. They used to be like stepping onto a cloud, but I think I would have done better to invest in a pair of Super Feet insoles for my old ones instead. These new gels are not as cushy or supportive as in the past. I'm sure the company went with cheaper materials. Next time, I will go with a new company.
reviews.author: AEC5IZ5JMOSOUXT5NDVAYV6JTL7Q

reviews.title: Upgraded from Nimbus 18 and Pleasantly Satisfied
reviews.text:
I wore the Nimbus 18 for years and absolutely loved them. However, as happens to all well-worn shoes, they started to fall apart. I naively thought that I could just re-purchase the same pair, but I couldn't find them for sale in my size anywhere. I saw that the Nimbus 19 and 20 were now available, so I started looking into both. A lot of the reviews on the 20 complained that they did not measure up to the quality of the Nimbus 16 and 18 (smaller toe box, not as comfortable), so I was hesitant to buy these, but I took a risk based on the reviews that said they were comparable (and I really didn't want to start researching any other brands).

I bought the Nimbus 20 in the same size as my Nimbus 18, and they fit perfectly. I do notice that the toe box is slightly more narrow than the 18, but it's not something that I've had any issues with since I got them a few weeks ago. The shoe is lighter than the 18, but does not seem to sacrifice cushion/comfort. I'm pleasantly satisfied and relieved that the 20 fit the bill for me!
reviews.author: AEKETMNT3NRZCR2E7SBAJFLDFZHQ

reviews.text: I have been a loyal user of Asics for years, but this shoe does not really work for me. I get a tightness and pain in the balls of my feet sometimes, and I can't figure out why. reviews.text: I LOVE THIS SHOE DUE TO THE FACT I HAD HARDWARE INSTALLED INTO MY RIGHT FOOT BECAUSE MY TOES HAVE COLLAPSED FROM RHEUMATOID ARTHRITIS .... THE ORTHOPEADIC DOCTOR RECOMMEND THIS BRAND ( RUNNING SHOE) SINCE I WORK ON CONCRETE FLOORS ..... THIS SHOE HELPS A GREAT DEAL FOR ME AND I HOPE IT HELPS SOMEONE ELSE GOING THROUGH THE SAME THING .... ALL THESE DIFFERENT KINDS OF GYM SHOES I BOUGHT AND NOTHING WORKS TIL THE DOCTOR RECOMMEND THIS BRAND OF SHOE. I'M NOT GETTING PAID TO SAY THIS, BUT I WILL SAY, IF YOU WERE IN MY POSITION TRYING TO FIND COMFORT AFTER SPENDING $$$$ FOR SEVERAL DIFFERENT TYPES OF GYM SHOES AND SOMEONE RECOMMEND A CERTAIN BRAND COMPARED TO ALL THE $$$$ YOU SPENT PRIOR .... I WISH I COULD GET MY MONEY BACK ON EVERY F*****  SINGLE (18) PAIR OF SHOES I PURCHASED ... FROM NOW ON THIS IS MY ONLY GYM SHOE I'LL WEAR FOR NOW ON.... LOVE THE COMFORT, WIDTH, AND I'M ABLE TO WALK MORE THAN 1 MILE WITHOUT HAVING TO STOP BECAUSE MY FEET ARE HURTING DUE TO THE CHRONIC  (RA) CONDITION LIFE THROWN AT ME.  LOVE LOVE LOVE THESE SHOES

title: Brooks Men's Ghost 14 Neutral Running Shoe
description: Smooth roads are ahead when you put on the Ghost 14. This award-winning runner favorite now features 100% DNA LOFT in the midsole, for a softer, smoother ride. We’ve also improved the fit so the shoe adjusts even more comfortably to your foot. THIS MEN'S SHOE IS FOR: Runners looking for a smooth ride that won’t distract from the fun of the run. The Ghost 14 offers a simplified midsole construction which creates more seamless transitions. This Brooks Ghost 14 is a certified PDAC A5500 Diabetic shoe and has been granted the APMA Seal of Acceptance. We recommend ordering Brooks running shoes 1/2 to one size larger than your dress shoe. SUPPORT AND CUSHION: The Ghost 14 offers neutral support while providing high energizing cushioning. Ideal for road running, cross training, the gym or wherever you might want to take them! Predecessor: Ghost 13 BALANCED, SOFT CUSHIONING: BioMoGo DNA and DNA LOFT cushioning work together to provide a just-right softness underfoot without losing responsiveness and durability - yet it feels lighter than ever. DNA LOFT now makes up the entire midsole, for a softer, smoother, ride. SMOOTH, STABLE RIDE: No matter how your foot lands, our Segmented Crash Pad - an integrated system of shock absorbers - will cushion every step and stride for smooth heel-to-toe transitions. SOFT, SECURE, FIT: More strategic use of the 3D Fit Print upper technology provides structure that moves with the foot.

reviews.title: Brooks has lost a step on wear/quality, but still a very good shoe
reviews.text:
First, Brooks is a top running shoe manufacturer, and I always like buying their products, and they are to be applauded for releasing shoes in widths, which lets you get a shoe that fits, which is essential. If a shoe does not fit properly, then it will be extremely difficult to run (or walk any distance in). The Brooks Glycerin is one model up from the Ghost, that has better cushioning, but is no longer available in a Narrow width, and is more expensive, but the Ghost is a pretty good shoe on its own. I would have given the Ghost 5 stars, but the Ghost 14 is not quite perfect, having two areas where it could be better (and was better previously).

The first picture shows a Ghost 14 (on the left), next to a Ghost 13 (the previous model, on the right), and you can see the Ghost 14 has less tread, so wears out sooner, I would say roughly 50-75 miles of road (asphalt) running. The picture shows both shoes new. The Ghost 13 also was a little more cushioned, but you can offset that some by putting a different insole in the 14, and a lot of runners replace the insoles anyway. The thinner tread means less mileage on the 14s, but still probably as good as most of the competition, just no longer better here.

The second pictures shows the upper collar on a Ghost 14, and the fabric Brooks is using there (not so much on the 13 and earlier models) is nice and soft, but does not hold up. I generally see holes or failures at the top of the lace area, and on some shoes, towards the back collar area. You get a lot of fraying, sometimes holes, which is more an appearance problem than anything that would impact running in them, but means also you cannot wear them casually, at least where someone might see the failures. That may be an aspect that is supply chain related, they used to hold up better, but I have seen the problem on two pair of Ghost 14s, and other manufacturers do a bit better (probably needs a bit more nylon, to hold up longer).

I will say that the Ghost 14 has a narrower tongue at the top, that is shared with the 13 but was new there, and I have not seen it much in other shoes, but actually works very well, so I give kudos to Brooks on that design innovation. There is less chance that during a run the tongue will shift over into the forefoot, at least in a painful way, and you don't have to stop to shift it back.

So overall, particularly at this price point, the Brooks continues to be good value and one of the top shoes to consider, but as with anything, there are a couple of areas that could use further improvement.
reviews.author: AHXZZRPP65AP7ACEAMVC75A4JNAQ

reviews.title: would pass
reviews.text:
You can check my order history. I've bought 13 different pairs of brooks shoes over the years(beast, ghost, transcend, etc). This newer version of the ghost just is a major step back.

The shoe is slightly narrower, has absolutely no cushion, less tread than my previous shoes, and squeaks with each step. The laces are cheaper and the top of the shoe is thinner. I'm returning these. This is a 35$ shoe being sold at 130$ range. No thanks.

After several faithful years buying brooks, I'm now trying their competitor's shoes.

edit: Bought Saucony's for 60$ that were so drastically better than this brooks shoe. I've moved on.

description: To create the next generation speed cross 5, we went back to the source code, capitalizing on the values that make speed cross legendary. Imported Rubber sole Trail Running Shoes: Featuring an aggressive grip, precise foothold, and protection, the Salomon Speedcross 5 is the ideal shoe for runners who want to conquer soft, technical trails Get Rough: Redesigned with deep, sharp lugs for an improved grip and a more dynamic upper for better fit and stability, the Speedcross 5 is the perfect shoe for trail runners who aren't afraid of wet, dirty terrain Go The Distance: Whether you love road running in your neighborhood, an escape to a local trail, or something more rugged, Salomon makes shoes that ensure you have a smooth ride on any and all terrain Time To Play: Whether you are into trail running, hiking, backpacking, or mountaineering, Salomon has shoes and gear for kids, men and women to help you conquer a new distance, personal record, or event Since 1947 Salomon has created innovative outdoor sports gear. reviews.title: Great alternative to cleats for some field sports
reviews.text:
As a 55 yo ulitimate frisbee and touch-football player these were a great find. They allowed totally adequate field traction while being not much heavier than cleats. The best part is the impact on my joints is much less with these shoes than if I'd have used cleats. The ankle support wasn't an issue either.

I use them for running/hiking as well and love them. The tread is lasting longer than most shoes I've purchases. The shoe tightening mechanism work well. The price is reasonable.

Love the color and look of the army green Speedcross 5's - I will just wear them casually when they get to worn for sports usage.

reviews.title: Crazy expensive for Made in Vietnam, but I like them.
reviews.text:
These shoes have a weight of 12oz each. Not super heavy, but not super lightweight either. Though you can see a thick white foam on the sole. . .The thickness of the bottom of the shoe is only about an 1/8 of an inch. If you’re running on a track or treadmill, that will probably not be an issue. But if you’re running on the road, I doubt the tread is going to last too long.

Padding on the inside is super-firm. Nice arch support. Nice firm padding around the heel / ankle.

Mesh breathable upper. The upper is all one piece, and the tongue is not separate. As you can from the pictures. . . .The laces are placed through plastic “shields” on each side of the shoe.

These shoes are Made in Vietnam. They are comfortable, and there is extra “bounce”, but I can’t say that they are worth the crazy expensive price. Nonetheless, I really like them.

reviews.title: I likes ‘em
reviews.text:
I’m not a runner so I can’t tell you what these or like for running in (though I’ve read good things about that), but I just wear them as every day, all around sneaks and they’re great for that. Great fit, cushy without making you feel like you’re going to fall over, and my previous pair of ghost 11s have held up nicely so I imagine these will too.

The instep on the shoes is just nylon mesh, so they’re anything but waterproof. They’re not even remotely water-resistant. But don’t expect that and I think you’ll probably be very happy with these shoes. Maybe not – different people’s feet are different and I’ve read a few reviews from people who aren’t thrilled with these. But for my part I’ve had a full range of shoes and sneakers over the years, some great and some not so much, and these are among the ones I’ve been happiest with. May you have a similar experience!

title: Nike Men's Sneakers
description: The versatile Nike SB Charge is a skateboarding sneaker with a simple style. Constructed with a canvas upper, they also sport a comfortable Solarsoft insole, round toe, and a vulcanized rubber sole. Rubber sole sku=843896-001-8.5

reviews.title: Good Shoe, but not for long hours
reviews.text: Good looking sneaker and I do enjoy wearing them. I have only had them for a couple of weeks now and the soul is already starting to peel away a little bit, I'm not sure they will last longer than a few months. They are comfortable to wear, but not for long hours.
reviews.author: AHB2EWDBA6T75WX3XMRYCQR5G3GQ

title: ASICS Men's Gel-Kayano 26 Running Shoes
description: Celebrate the retro style of the historic Japanese capital in this special edition GEL-KAYANO 26 Retro Tokyo running shoe from ASICS. This updated version combines advanced components with a striking new design that honors Tokyo's heritage. The GEL-KAYANO 26 shoe allows you to tackle long distances in comfort, thanks to the rearfoot GEL technology that's been redesigned for improved shock absorption upon impact. You'll also benefit from a bouncy feel that stems from the FLYTEFOAM Propel midsole technology. The DUOMAX technology in the midsole offers better support and helps resist excessive overpronation while I.G.S. technology promotes smoother propulsion. Additionally, this style is accented with reflective detailing to improve your visibility when running in low light settings. Get the inspiration and support you need to smash your training goals in the special edition GEL-KAYANO 26 Retro Tokyo performance running shoe from ASICS. Synthetic-and-mesh

reviews.title: Super shoes— my 6th pair!
reviews.text: I get a new pair every year for birthday and absolutely LOVE them ! Great fit, fine look, they seem to get better every year!!!

reviews.title: Asics Kayano
reviews.text: Asics Kayano's are the best!  Lots of support in the heel, and the Duomax support system is awesome!
reviews.author: AEHZKXBLTVWU4MYNYB76YSSIDZLQ

reviews.title: Update:Tread showing considerable wear after 70 miles
reviews.text:
Two star: Upper is holding up nicely. Tread is already worn out after only 70 miles. I tried ASICS because reviews talk of superior durability. So far for me upper is good, sole is not.

Update: I reached out to ASICS about the tread life. After sending pictures of tread wear after 70 miles they sent code for replacement shoe from ASICS.com.
That’s pretty awesome customer service in my book.

So far 20 miles on new Kayano fit and finish is great.

Comfort on gel-Kayano 28 is far superior to previous models.

Extra wide is a touch narrow but all in all the first 20 miles is running great.
reviews.author: AGGMVQZYTHFID446RLZE4CN7P2RA

reviews.title: He’ll only wear ASICS
reviews.text: My husband buys one pair of shoes a year (imagine that!). Of course, he wears them every single day—365 days! ASICS are the best fit and most comfortable for him. They’re fit true to size and are just as advertised. He’s very happy!
reviews.author: AEYBNGQ7VMN2PI2PAPVN7V4OKBUA

title: Brooks Womens Glycerin 18 Running Shoe
description: A bit like running in the clouds. Thanks to the increased elasticity of the upper and the super-soft cushioning throughout the length, these men's running shoes are the ultimate comfort. Super soft cushioning. The revised ratio between midsole and outer sole allows you to increase LOFT DNA to achieve even more extreme softness at the sole of the foot, without losing reactivity or endurance. An OrthoLiteTM insole ensures an extraordinary wearing comfort. Imported Rubber sole THIS WOMEN'S SHOE IS FOR: The Glycerin 18 is perfect for runners who think there's no such thing as too much cushioning. The upper enhances comfort by perfectly balancing stretch and structure. SUPPORT AND CUSHION: Provides neutral support while offering the maximum amount of cushioning. Ideal for road running, cross training, the gym or wherever you might want to take them! Predecessor: Glycerin 17. SUPER-SOFT CUSHIONING: Increased DNA LOFT super-soft cushioning allows for even more extreme softness, without losing responsiveness or durability, while the OrthoLite sockliner provides premium step-in comfort. PLUSH FIT: The plush feel of an internal stretch bootie surrounds your foot and moves and expands with your stride. The engineered mesh upper enhances the fit. SMOOTH TRANSITIONS: The plush transition zone makes every move from heel to toe feel incredibly soft and smooth.

reviews.title: Defective manufacturing
reviews.text: I’m this was my second pair of these shoes within 6 months but this pair is very u comfortable on the left shoe and I can’t do walks in them.  Very disappointed in it... it’s like the left shoe has a defect I it.  Unfortunately I saved the shoe for over a month before I wore it and too late to return.

reviews.title: Great support
reviews.text: These are very comfortable and supportive. I started wearing Brooks when I got planter fasciitis. I haven’t had issues in a long time. I tried another model, but I went back to these.
reviews.author: AHCGZKIIVMI4LXTY755T56AM7IXA

reviews.title: My Shoe!
reviews.text: These are the only shoes I have been wearing on a daily basis for over 10 years, Brooks Glycerin. My feet have seen many years of 12 hour shifts and are in sad shape. I have a dress pair, a pair I wear indoors, and several pairs for outdoors and yard work.  I can wear a man's 8 1/2 D or a Women's 9 1/2 wide and they fit perfectly.  If you need a cushioned shoe with good support these are the ones to buy.
reviews.author: AHT6JPVNDI234TQRQ77HEBEKR47A

reviews.text: The shoes are NOT worth 130.00 or 50.00 inserts last less than one month shoes themselves no arch support. In the end caused my feet problems.
